video:video.video:title: Introducing Money Happens: A new AP audio series
video:video.video:description: Listen to Money Happens, a new audio series where the AP follows how people are dealing with debt, housing and rising costs and provides practical tips from financial experts

video:video.video:title: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?
video:video.video:description: What did you think of the Met Gala fashion this year?

video:video.video:title: Kneecap 'thrive in the chaos' with album 'Fenian' shaped by court case | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The Irish hip-hop group Kneecap recorded their second album "Fenian" while dealing with a legal battle. Liam Óg Ó hAnnaidh, known as Mo Chara, faced terror-related charges for allegedly displaying a Hezbollah flag during a London concert.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish premieres concert film with James Cameron — and confetti
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish and James Cameron pose at the Los Angeles premiere of her "Hit Me Hard and Soft" concert film — then the confetti hits.

video:video.video:title: With new album on the way, Shania Twain hosts ACM Awards | AP interview
video:video.video:description: With an album on the way, Shania Twain is hosting the Academy of Country Music Awards in Las Vegas on May 17.

video:video.video:title: Watch a dramatic thunderstorm sweep across northwestern Kansas
video:video.video:description: Timelapse footage shows a thunderstorm as it sweeps across northwestern Kansas.

video:video.video:title: Why many adopted kids end up in residential treatment centers, an AP investigation
video:video.video:description: An Associated Press investigation finds a business known for tough-love boarding schools for rebellious teenagers has set its sights on a different demographic: adopted kids.

video:video.video:title: Ziggy Marley on legacy, philanthropy and new album | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Ziggy Marley is looking to the "Brightside." The Grammy-winning 57-year-old musician focused his ninth solo studio album on optimism and mental health, along with nods to legacy.

video:video.video:title: Cannes Film Festival opens with Moore, Jackson, Fonda on red carpet
video:video.video:description: The 2026 Cannes Film Festival kicked off with a red carpet featuring Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård, Jane Fonda, Tyrese Gibson and director Peter Jackson, who was given an honorary Palme d’Or at the opening ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Cannes 2026: Top moments from opening day
video:video.video:description: The 79th Cannes Film Festival launched on Tuesday, marking the start of 12 days of nonstop premieres that will culminate May 23 with the presentation of the prestigious Palme d’Or.

video:video.video:title: 'Dirtnado' sweeps through Minnesota farm
video:video.video:description: A "dirtnado" was captured sweeping through a northern Montana farm.

video:video.video:title: The Beach Boys on 60 years of 'Pet Sounds' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: The surviving members of The Beach Boys — Mike Love, Al Jardine and Bruce Johnston — reunited at the iconic Capitol Records building in Los Angeles to celebrate the 60th anniversary of "Pet Sounds," the landmark album that was released May 16, 1966.

video:video.video:title: Watch the moment a cuttlefish changes color
video:video.video:description: Underwater footage captures the moment a cuttlefish changed colors from brown to a bright yellow off the coast of Sydney.

video:video.video:title: Cartel drone attacks empty Mexican towns, force hundreds to flee
video:video.video:description: Alleged members of Los Ardillos attacked communities in Mexico's Guerrero with gunfire, arson and drone explosives, forcing hundreds to flee.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Two Navy jets collide during Idaho air show
video:video.video:description: An air show in Idaho was called off when two Navy jets collided and crashed. The four crew members from both jets safely ejected and the crash is under investigation.

video:video.video:title: Police on Long Island deploy pepper spray on crowd lining up for high demand luxury watch
video:video.video:description: A scuffle erupted between deputies and watch enthusiasts who had camped outside a New York store on Saturday, prompting officers to deploy pepper spray on the crowd.

video:video.video:title: Venus Williams sparkles on 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Venus Williams sparkles in a black gown on the 2026 carpet.

video:video.video:title: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar meets Meloni in Rome
video:video.video:description: Hungary’s newly-elected prime minister Péter Magyar met his Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni in Rome on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: What to know about hantavirus and how it spreads
video:video.video:description: Hantaviruses exist throughout the world and are spread mainly by contact with rodents. It's rare and not easy for a hantavirus to spread between people.

video:video.video:title: US teen Buddhist lama is now a monk studying in the Himalayan foothills
video:video.video:description: Jalue Dorje grew up a typical American teen, loving video games and football. But he's also an aspiring spiritual leader recognized from an early age by the Dalai Lama as a reincarnated lama.

video:video.video:title: Beer, brass and 'Hau-Ruck' shouts as a Bavarian village raises its new maypole
video:video.video:description: It's May, and in the Bavarian village of Kühbach, residents are marking the first day of the month by raising a single decorated tree trunk known as a maypole, a custom observed across southern Germany, Austria and parts of central Europe.

video:video.video:title: Trump declines to say if he and Xi discussed Taiwan
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump arrived at the Temple of Heaven with Chinese President Xi Jinping after the pair discussed trade, Taiwan and other differences in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Tennessee man known for posting racist videos gets $1.25 million bond on attempted murder charge
video:video.video:description: A Tennessee man who goes by Chud the Builder and is known for posting racist videos has been given a $1.25 million preliminary bond on attempted murder charges.

video:video.video:title: With so many aging influencers are we in an era of golden old age?
video:video.video:description: Old age is supposed to be when we step back and relax. But it seems many are stepping forward. If you look online, or in the media, in politics, entertainment or business older people appear to be at their peak.

video:video.video:title: Preparations underway at Granadilla port in Tenerife for arrival of hantavirus-stricken ship
video:video.video:description: Spanish authorities were on Saturday preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands, where health officials have said they will perform careful evacuations.

video:video.video:title: Higher costs force Mississippi farmer to raise prices
video:video.video:description: Foot Print Farms CEO Cindy Ayers-Elliott tries to keep her produce affordable, but she has been forced to raise prices as she struggles to make a profit amid higher costs for gas, fertilizer and other essential items.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberations begin in murder retrial of former Ohio deputy who fatally shot Black man
video:video.video:description: A jury in Ohio has begun deliberating in the retrial of former sheriff's deputy Jason Meade, charged with murder and reckless homicide in the fatal shooting of 23-year-old Casey Goodson Jr.

video:video.video:title: Paul McCartney and Wings members' artifacts on display at exhibit at Rock and Roll Hall of Fame
video:video.video:description: The largest collection of Paul McCartney's personal artifacts ever publicly displayed is part of a new exhibit at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in Cleveland, centering on his life after the Beatles and his band Wings.

video:video.video:title: Cuba president condemns additional sanctions imposed by Trump administration
video:video.video:description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has rejected the latest sanctions imposed by the U.S. government against the island — and against anyone who dares to trade with it — and questioned how long the world will continue to tolerate what he considered to be an abusive collective punishment by the U.S. ad

video:video.video:title: Trump to undergo medical checkup as scrutiny over his health continues
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is scheduled to undergo a medical and dental checkup later this month, as the White House says the president continues routine preventive care ahead of turning 80 in June.

video:video.video:title: Princess of Wales visits Italy after announcing her cancer was in remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish joined by Nat Wolff at premiere
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish describes her relationship with older songs, as she joins Nat Wolff, brother Finneas and co-director James Cameron at the premiere her new tour documentary.

video:video.video:title: A new album and intention for country artist Ashley McBryde
video:video.video:description: Country artist Ashley McBryde suffered a severe injury in 2021, falling from a horse in Montana and landing on her head.

video:video.video:title: The top photos of the week by Associated Press Photojournalists
video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 24-30 April 2026

video:video.video:title: Hawaii's Kilauea volcano erupts again
video:video.video:description: Kilauea has periodically sent fountains of lava soaring during more than a year of an on-and-off eruption.

video:video.video:title: Ridglan Beagles find fans in pop star Debbie Gibson and Black Sabbath bass player
video:video.video:description: Pop star Debbie Gibson and Terry “Geezer” Butler, co-founder of the heavy metal band Black Sabbath, praised ongoing efforts to find new homes for roughly 1,500 beagles purchased from dog breeder and research facility Ridglan Farms outside of Madison.

video:video.video:title: Macron sings at Armenian state dinner with Pashinyan on drums
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron took to the microphone at a state dinner in Yerevan on Monday, with Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan accompanying him on the drums.

video:video.video:title: Iranian news agency posts new video said to show still closed Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Iran’s Fars news agency posted a video on Monday showing vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. According to a Fars correspondent, the video shows ships in the northern part of the strait, which is still closed.

video:video.video:title: IDF releases video of what it says is an operation to dismantle Gaza tunnel routes
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Friday that its troops had “dismantled eight underground tunnel routes” spanning more than 2.5 kilometers that were used by Hamas in the southern Gaza Strip.

video:video.video:title: 'I consider myself lucky': Survivor recounts Modena car attack
video:video.video:description: A man injured in a car-ramming and stabbing attack in Italy’s northern city of Modena said on Monday he considered himself lucky not to have been more seriously injured, and thanked those who came to his aid.

video:video.video:title: Torrential rains trigger severe flooding in district near Turkey’s Black Sea coast
video:video.video:description: The heavy rainfall late on Tuesday caused rivers in the Havza district of Samsun province to overflow, sending waters rushing down streets, carrying vehicles and debris.

video:video.video:title: Iran calls US proposal to the UN on Strait of Hormuz 'flawed' and 'politically motivated'
video:video.video:description: The resolution -- put forth by the U.S. and Bahrain -- is the second attempt to punish Iran for leveraging its hold over the vital waterway since U.S. and Israeli strikes ignited the war in late February.

video:video.video:title: Protesters rally at Alabama Senate as lawmakers weigh redistricting legislation
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a hallway outside the Alabama state Senate in Montgomery on Wednesday. Legislation authorizing special Congressional primaries moved on to the Alabama Senate, after the state House passed it.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Rubio says he hopes China talks to Iran about opening the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Tuesday he hopes Chinese officials talk to Iran about the Strait of Hormuz during a foreign minister's visit this week.

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga arrives at 'Mayhem Requiem' premiere
video:video.video:description: Lady Gaga arrives to screening of “Apple Music Live: Lady Gaga Mayhem Requiem.”

video:video.video:title: 'There is no ceasefire': Displaced people in Beirut unconvinced by extension to Israel-Hezbollah truce
video:video.video:description: Displaced families living in makeshift camps in Beirut remain skeptical about the 45-day extension of the expiring Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Filipino girl group BINI say they're 'blessed' to represent their culture
video:video.video:description: BINI say they're "blessed" to "proudly represent" their culture after making history as the first Filipino girl group to perform at Coachella.
video:video.video:tag: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival

video:video.video:title: AI agent given the chance to manage a cafe in Stockholm, but it's still fine tuning the bread order
video:video.video:description: The coffee might be poured by a human hand, but behind the counter something far less traditional is calling the shots at a new experimental cafe in Stockholm.

video:video.video:title: United Airlines plane strikes a moving truck during landing at Newark
video:video.video:description: A low flying United Airlines plane crashed in a light pole and a moving bakery truck on the New Jersey Turnpike Sunday afternoon after making an attempt to land at Newark Airport.

video:video.video:title: Paul Bettany and Will Sharpe revisit 'Amadeus'
video:video.video:description: Will Sharpe plays Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart in new Starz limited series, "Amadeus." Paul Bettany plays his nemesis, Antonio Salieri, whose envy and bitter obsession with Mozart saw him turn against his friend and colleague.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong court hears final arguments in former Tiananmen vigil organizers' national security trial
video:video.video:description: Chow Hang-tung and Lee Cheuk-yan, two former leaders of the Hong Kong Alliance in Support of Patriotic Democratic Movements of China, have pleaded not guilty.

video:video.video:title: US health officials detail plans for American passengers on hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: The U.S. agreed to send a plane to repatriate and quarantine about 17 Americans who are still on the ship who were on board the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: World Cup pitch at Dallas stadium unveiled
video:video.video:description: Officials on Wednesday unveiled the newly installed World Cup pitch inside Dallas Stadium in Arlington, Texas where suspended LED grow lights are being used to help maintain the grass field ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup matches.

video:video.video:title: New York City police release footage of machete-wielding man shot at Grand Central
video:video.video:description: New York City police have released body-worn camera footage of officers shooting and killing a machete-wielding man who stabbed three people at a Grand Central subway stop last month.

video:video.video:title: Protests in Venezuela after elderly mother dies days after learning missing son died in prison
video:video.video:description: Protesters demonstrated Monday in Venezuela’s capital in memory of a woman who died over the weekend, just days after learning that her son had died in state custody 10 months ago.

video:video.video:title: Landlords seek relief from unpaid rent in lawsuit against federal eviction ban
video:video.video:description: A group of landlords are hoping to settle with the federal government over what they say are billions of dollars in losses due to the federal eviction moratorium in place for part of the pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Putin: Russia's conflict with Ukraine 'coming to an end'
video:video.video:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin said a U.S.-brokered ceasefire with Ukraine had emerged following discussions with the United States, following the Russian Ministry of Defense's statement about the possibility of retaliatory attacks on Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Two suspects killed in shooting at a San Diego mosque
video:video.video:description: The two suspects in a Monday shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego are dead, according to a police source.

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Mid-term elections in Britain could hasten the end for embattled leader Starmer
video:video.video:description: British voters will cast their ballots on Thursday in elections that could hasten the end of Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s troubled term, and confirm that an increasingly dis-United Kingdom has entered an era of messy multiparty politics.

video:video.video:title: Large fire spreads across Chernobyl exclusion zone as landmines hamper firefighting efforts
video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants peace deal to end war in the Middle East 'more than I do'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump told reporters in Washington that the ceasefire with Iran was holding. Trump also said Iran "trifled with us," after the U.S. military said it intercepted Iranian attacks on three Navy ships in the Strait of Hormuz and “targeted Iranian military facilities responsible for atta

video:video.video:title: Eilish calls Attenborough 'the GOAT'
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish explains why she admires David Attenborough - who will turn 100 this week - as she promotes her concert movie with iconic director James Cameron.

video:video.video:title: Naming of new species among scores of tributes honoring David Attenborough's 100th birthday
video:video.video:description: A newly discovered parasitic wasp from Chile has been named after Sir David Attenborough ahead of his 100th birthday, one of several tributes marking the broadcaster’s influence on natural history, conservation and environmental awareness.

video:video.video:title: Cruise ship waits for help after suspected outbreak of rare hantavirus onboard kills 3
video:video.video:description: At least three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: A converted church in rural Pennsylvania is becoming an incubator for Amish roots music
video:video.video:description: Ragamuffin Hall in McCoysville was the setting last weekend for a concert by two of the rising stars, Ben and Rose Stoltzfus.

video:video.video:title: 14-year-old boy among 6 dead from Honduras and Mexico found in hot Texas shipping container
video:video.video:description: More details are out after six people were found dead in a rail yard shipping container in Laredo, Texas, last weekend.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine's LELÉKA connects with fans ahead of Eurovision's semifinal
video:video.video:description: Eurovision contestant LELÉKA met with members of her native Ukrainian community in Vienna to connect with fans.

video:video.video:title: World Health Organization expert says hantavirus is not the next COVID
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is not like the COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Floral festival takes over wealthy London neighbourhood
video:video.video:description: Shops and public spaces in London's affluent neighborhood are decorated in blooms for an annual celebration that runs parallel to the world-famous Chelsea Flower Show.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo marks World Press Freedom Day remembering members of media killed at war
video:video.video:description: The United Nations General Assembly proclaimed May 3 as World Press Freedom Day in 1993. 2025 was the deadliest year for journalists worldwide ever recorded, according to The Committee to Protect Journalists.

video:video.video:title: Shooting at San Diego mosque kills 3 men and both suspects are dead, police say
video:video.video:description: A shooting at a San Diego mosque on Monday killed three men, and two teenage suspects are also dead, the police chief said.

video:video.video:title: Indiana woman formerly prescribed Mifepristone reacts to Supreme Court abortion pill decision
video:video.video:description: For Kristina Moorhead, a woman in Indiana who was prescribed the pills twice to complete two missed miscarriages in 2024, the decision “is incredibly important”.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: 'Is God Is' writer and director Aleshea Harris on how the film explores unapologetic female rage
video:video.video:description: Award-winning playwright Aleshea Harris is bringing "Is God Is" to the screen. The film follows two twin sisters, Racine and Anaia, who embark on a quest for revenge to confront their dark family history.

video:video.video:title: Nine people injured in Russian attack on Kharkiv
video:video.video:description: Nine people were injured, including three children, in a Russian strike on Ukraine’s second largest city, Kharkiv, on Thursday, according to Kharkiv Mayor Ihor Terekhov.

video:video.video:title: A fan-run club pushes back against aggressive stadium culture in Poland
video:video.video:description: A grassroots Warsaw soccer club formed by fans more than a decade ago to resist aggressive nationalist stadium culture is hoping to find new relevance in Poland — a country whose president doesn’t deny his own past participation in fights between football fans.

video:video.video:title: Barbara Palvin shows off baby bump with Dylan Sprouse in Cannes
video:video.video:description: Actor Dylan Sprouse and his model wife Barbara Palvin are expecting their first baby. The couple announced the news at the 79th Annual Cannes Film Festival. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: Atlanta Police Department holds mass shooting training ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: The Atlanta Police department is preparing its emergency response in case an incident happens during the World Cup. (AP Video by Emilie Megnien)

video:video.video:title: Brazilian artisan crafts replica World Cup trophies to the joy of locals and celebrities alike
video:video.video:description: Brazilian soccer fan and former metallurgist Jarbas Meneghini Carlini has been combining his love for the sport with his business, making replicas of the FIFA World Cup trophy in his workshop in western Rio de Janeiro.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors to retry Alex Murdaugh after murder convictions overturned
video:video.video:description: Alex Murdaugh’s murder convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son were overturned Wednesday by the South Carolina Supreme Court.

video:video.video:title: Jimmy Lai's son hopes Trump's meeting with Xi could lead to breakthrough to free his father
video:video.video:description: Ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's anticipated trip to Beijing next week, the family of imprisoned media mogul and Hong Kong's pro-democracy activist Jimmy Lai was hopeful of his release, Sebastien Lai, son of Jimmy Lai, said in an interview with The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Iron Maiden on their mascot, Eddie
video:video.video:description: British heavy metal veterans Iron Maiden love to hate their terrifying mascot, Eddie.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Sunday, May 17th: WHO declares an Ebola emergency; A drone strike hits the UAE’s sole nuclear plant; Russian officials say Ukrainian strikes killed four people; An owl sanctuary in Spain promotes wildlife conservation.

video:video.video:title: What to know about new Ebola outbreak that has killed scores of people in Congo
video:video.video:description: Africa’s top public health body has confirmed a new Ebola outbreak in Congo's Ituri province. There have been 246 suspected cases and 65 deaths so far.

video:video.video:title: States across the wildfire-prone Western U.S. are using AI for early detection
video:video.video:description: AI cameras are playing an increasingly important role in detecting wildfires in the drought-prone U.S. West.

video:video.video:title: Colombia discusses hippo relocation with Vantara after backlash over euthanasia plan
video:video.video:description: The Colombian government on Wednesday announced that Indian conservation initiative Vantara had expressed interest in relocating an invasive population of hippopotamuses in the country.

video:video.video:title: Power outages and fuel shortages deepen frustrations across Cuba
video:video.video:description: Residents in Havana banged pots and pans overnight Thursday into Friday as prolonged power outages and fuel shortages deepened frustrations across Cuba.

video:video.video:title: A Latino grocery store in Delaware turns its produce aisle into a music stage
video:video.video:description: A family-owned Latino grocery store in Delaware is attracting online audiences and bands from far away with monthly concerts from diverse artists and genres.

video:video.video:title: Thousands of volunteers build dozens of homes in a week in Atlanta with Habitat for Humanity
video:video.video:description: Over five days, nearly 2,200 volunteers with the international nonprofit will finish building 24 new housing units in Atlanta along with homeowners.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in the Netherlands for disinfection as final crew disembark
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak docked at the Dutch port of Rotterdam for disinfection Monday, wrapping up a troubled journey that put international health authorities on alert.

video:video.video:title: Russia ramps up attempts to kill opponents in Europe, intelligence officials say
video:video.video:description: While Russian officials have long been accused of silencing the country’s enemies abroad, three Western intelligence officials from different countries told AP that a campaign of targeted killings has ramped up since President Vladimir Putin's 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Groups rally to defend Black political representation in Alabama
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people gathered in Montgomery, Alabama, to mobilize support for voting rights amid concerns about states that are moving to dismantle congressional districts that expanded representation opportunities.

video:video.video:title: Small medical plane crashes in New Mexico, killing all 4 people aboard
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, May 5th: Defense department says ceasefire with Iran still in effect; Romanian government falls as no-confidence motion against Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan passes; Book publishers sue Meta; Tony Award nominations announced.

video:video.video:title: Meet Tornado and Twister, the two kittens rescued after tornado in Mississippi
video:video.video:description: Two aptly-named kittens, Tornado and Twister, who were rescued from the rubble of a tornado-flattened Mississippi trailer park, now have a new family.

video:video.video:title: High-speed passenger hydrofoil boat makes its first seasonal voyage from Sochi to Sukhumi
video:video.video:description: A high-speed passenger vessel "Kometa" made its first voyage of the year on Friday from Russia's Sochi to Sukhumi in Abkhazia, Georgia.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 18
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 18th: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship docks in Rotterdam; Luigi Mangione in court for evidence hearing; Turkey's Erdogan denounces Israeli navy's interception of Gaza-bound flotilla; Epstein files exhibition opens in New York City.

video:video.video:title: Drones, bullets and cartel warfare fuel an invisible displacement crisis in Mexico
video:video.video:description: María Cabrera and her family fled into the night-cloaked mountains of central Mexico with only the clothes on their backs when bombs fell from the sky and bullets ricocheted off her concrete floors.

video:video.video:title: Trump offers platitudes while Xi warns of possible confrontation during China summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has called Chinese President Xi Jinping a friend, but his warm words were in stark contrast to Xi, who opened a bilateral meeting by cautioning about potential conflict between the U.S. and China.

video:video.video:title: Hospitals in southern Lebanon face uphill struggle as they treat the wounded and displaced
video:video.video:description: Hospitals in southern Lebanon are under immense strain and overwhelmed due to the dual pressures of war injuries and displacement. (AP Video: Bassam Hatoum

video:video.video:title: Convoy of oil trucks shows war-battered Syria selling itself as safe corridor amid regional conflict
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of oil-laden trucks, some arriving from Iraq, lined up along Syria's coastal road on Friday as they wait to unload into tankers at Baniyas port.

video:video.video:title: Kneecap sampled court case crowds on new record
video:video.video:description: How Kneecap member Mo Chara's recent court case helped to inspire a song on the Irish rappers' second album, "Fenian."

video:video.video:title: Interfaith march in Jerusalem's Old City seeks to provide show of unity
video:video.video:description: Dozens of activists participated in an interfaith march through Jerusalem on Monday. (AP Video by Shlomo Mor)

video:video.video:title: Port Authority in New York tests drone deliveries between Manhattan and Brooklyn
video:video.video:description: The trial targets a year-end goal of delivering medical supplies, and eventually expanding to other routes. (AP video by Emily Wang Fujiyama)

video:video.video:title: Funeral held in Gaza for leader of Hamas’ military wing killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Mourners in the Gaza Strip held a funeral procession for the leader of Hamas’ military wing on Saturday after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike the previous day.

video:video.video:title: Why is everything so expensive? Business basics: Inflation
video:video.video:description: When the cost of nearly everything is rising, economists call it inflation. Even if paychecks also rise, inflation typically leads people feel like it's harder to make ends meet, and it often becomes a major political issue

video:video.video:title: Police search for suspects in Oklahoma shooting that sent at least 18 people to hospitals
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Oklahoma were looking for suspects Monday in a weekend shooting at an unsanctioned lakeside party packed with young adults that left at least 23 people injured, some critically, according to police and hospital officials.

video:video.video:title: A$AP Rocky on the Met Gala carpet: 'We feel how we look'
video:video.video:description: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky made a joint arrival at the Met Gala, closing out the carpet in signature fashion. Reflecting on their look, Rocky said, “We feel how we look.”

video:video.video:title: Protesters light bonfires during public transport strike in Kenya over fuel prices
video:video.video:description: Protests have erupted in Nairobi as a nationwide public transport strike begins over rising fuel prices. (AP video shot by Andrew Kasuku)

video:video.video:title: Israeli drone strikes hit highway south of Beirut, killing at least 8 people
video:video.video:description: Israeli drones struck three vehicles on the main highway south of Beirut Wednesday, killing eight people, including a woman and her two children, the Lebanese Health Ministry said.

video:video.video:title: Blunt and Tucci get stars on Walk of Fame
video:video.video:description: Emily Blunt and Stanley Tucci are celebrated by Matt Damon, Robert Downey Jr., Meryl Streep, and Dwayne Johnson, on the Hollywood Walk of Fame

video:video.video:title: WHO says hantavirus risk remains low globally, confirms eight cases and three deaths
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization (WHO) said Wednesday samples taken from a cruise ship that is in the midst of an outbreak of hantavirus had tested positive for the Andes strain of the virus.

video:video.video:title: Kouri Richins, who wrote a children’s book on grief after her husband’s death, gets life sentence
video:video.video:description: Kouri Richins was convicted in March of aggravated murder for lacing her husband Eric Richins’ cocktail with five times the lethal dose of fentanyl at their home near Park City in 2022.

video:video.video:title: At least five people killed and 38 injured in mall fire in northwestern Mexico
video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and another 38 injured on Thursday after a fire broke out at a shopping mall in the Mexican city of Los Mochis, state authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to visit White House in September during banquet toast in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and U.S. President Donald Trump each proposed a toast during a state dinner at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza say mass displacement suffered by their predecessors on Nakba Day continues today
video:video.video:description: Israeli bombardment and mass displacement in Gaza have left many Palestinians comparing the current war to the Nakba of 1948, when hundreds of thousands were forced from their homes during the creation of Israel.

video:video.video:title: Gigi Hadid shares what the Met Gala means to her
video:video.video:description: Model Gigi Hadid tells AP about the Meta Gala and fashion education.

video:video.video:title: Long DACA renewal wait times leave some 'Dreamers' without status, a job and fearing detainment
video:video.video:description: Renewal wait times for the Deferred Action for Childhood Arrivals program that allows people who were brought to the U.S. as children to temporarily remain in the country and work have increased dramatically in the past year.

video:video.video:title: Families evacuated from Gaza enjoy a day to decompress at Rome's ancient baths
video:video.video:description: The Baths of Caracalla, the sumptuously decorated public baths complex near the Colosseum, were long a place of leisure, health care and relaxation for ancient Romans.

video:video.video:title: At least one dead in Russian strike on kindergarten in Ukraine's Sumy
video:video.video:description: One person was killed and two injured in a Russian strike on a kindergarten in in Ukraine's northeastern city of Sumy on Wednesday, Ukrainian State Emergency Service said.

video:video.video:title: Health officials track dozens who left hantavirus-stricken ship after first fatality
video:video.video:description: Health officials in several countries are trying to identify and follow people who may have been exposed to hantavirus on a cruise ship. (Production by Elaine Carroll)

video:video.video:title: A handful of women who never heard of handball now are training to join the US Olympic team
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of women have tried out for a chance to join the U.S. women’s handball team for the 2028 Olympics.

video:video.video:title: How 'Breaking Bad' helped Nicolas Cage see the possibilities in television
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage says working on “Spider-Noir” as a series gave him more creative freedom than film. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Yuji the baby monkey finds comfort in plush teddy
video:video.video:description: Yuji, the baby patas monkey, is winning hearts and fans for his love of a stuffed teddy bear. Little Yuji was rejected by his mother at birth who was not able to care for him, so now he is receiving the love and care from the expert staff at Guadalajara Zoo in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Justice Department seeks whistleblowers to combat antitrust in beef industry
video:video.video:description: Top officials in the U.S. Justice Department and Department of Agriculture said the administration is seeking whistleblowers to combat anti-competitive practices in the beef industry.

video:video.video:title: Stars hit the red carpet for Cannes opening
video:video.video:description: The Cannes Film Festival kicked off Tuesday, with stars on the red carpet including Demi Moore, Stellan Skarsgård, Jane Fonda and Tyrese Gibson, along with director Peter Jackson, who was given an honorary Palme d’Or at the opening ceremony.

video:video.video:title: Ford is back with employee pricing for all as gas and car prices continue to climb
video:video.video:description: Ford Motor Co. is offering employee pricing to customers, as gas prices in the U.S. hit new multiyear highs and the cost of a new vehicle hovers near $50,000 (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: DRC health minister, WHO representatives arrive in Bunia with supplies to fight Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: DRC health minister Samuel Roger Kamba said on Sunday that authorities had already selected sites in Rwampara and Mongwalu to establish new treatment centers and that dozens of patients were receiving active care.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 11th: President Trump wants to suspend the federal tax on gas; Hantavirus exposed cruise ship passengers fly home; Heat stroke may have killed six people by Texas border; U.S.

video:video.video:title: A solar-powered charging station in central Cuba brings life to a darkened island
video:video.video:description: A free, solar-powered charging station, or “solinera” as it’s known, has opened in Santa Clara, in Cuba’s central region.

video:video.video:title: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs is found not guilty of assaulting his private chef
video:video.video:description: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs has been found not guilty of assaulting his private chef in a pay dispute.

video:video.video:title: Thai emergency services at scene of crash between cargo train and bus in Bangkok
video:video.video:description: A train crashed into a public bus on Saturday in Thailand’s capital, Bangkok, killing at least eight people.

video:video.video:title: Latvian prime minister resigns after controversy over stray Ukrainian drones
video:video.video:description: Latvian center-right Prime Minister Evika Silina announced her resignation on Thursday, after the Progressives Party, her left-leaning coalition partner, pulled support from the government and left her without a majority.

video:video.video:title: Congressman promises to sue over Tennessee's new US House map
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steve Cohen and the NAACP Memphis Branch are promising to fight the new U.S. House map that carves up Memphis' majority-Black congressional district and was approved by Republican lawmakers on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer comments after partial results show losses for Labour, wins for Reform UK in local elections
video:video.video:description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer insisted he would carry on as prime minister despite his Labour party suffering disastrous local election losses.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore, Lucas Bravo among guests at Women in Cinema Gala
video:video.video:description: The Red Sea Film Foundation's annual gala in Cap d'Antibes, South of France, honored Laïla Marrakchi, Genevieve Nnaji, Tara Sutaria, Marie-Clementine Dusabejambo, Kamila Andani and Aixa Kay.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson makes rare appearance at Grammy Hall of Fame Gala
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson made a rare appearance at the 2026 Grammy Hall of Fame Gala, where her album "Rhythm Nation 1814" was inducted.

video:video.video:title: Halsey is turning her attention to film
video:video.video:description: Grammy-nominated artist Halsey explains why she's at the Cannes Film Festival, including to network for her freshly announced horror, "Replacer." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Planned Parenthood: Goal of mail-order abortion pill ban is akin to a 'national abortion ban'
video:video.video:description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone asked the Supreme Court to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug in what was the biggest jolt to abortion policy since the overturning of Roe v.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Health official in Ushuaia doubts Tierra del Fuego is source of suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A health official in Ushuaia expressed doubts that Tierra del Fuego was the source of a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship with nearly 150 people on aboard, after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill.

video:video.video:title: San Diego police say they are responding to an active shooter at a local Islamic Center
video:video.video:description: San Diego police are responding to an active shooter at the Islamic Center of San Diego. Officer Anthony Carrasco says they believe people have been shot.

video:video.video:title: Historic Maryland racetrack will host its final races, including Preakness, before shutting down
video:video.video:description: Once home to packed grandstands and stars like Secretariat, Laurel now draws only a few hundred regulars on many race days.

video:video.video:title: A political dynasty heiress and a former trade minister advance to Peru’s presidential runoff, final vote tally shows
video:video.video:description: Conservative Keiko Fujimori and progressive Roberto Sánchez will compete for Peru’s presidency in a June 7 runoff election, according to the final vote count from an election held more than a month ago.

video:video.video:title: Owl found in concrete mixer is on the mend and maybe a bit wiser from ordeal
video:video.video:description: An adolescent owl that was found stuck in a concrete mixer in southwestern Utah is finally on the mend, flying free and maybe a bit wiser from the ordeal.

video:video.video:title: French hantavirus patient has most severe cardiopulmonary form, doctor says
video:video.video:description: A French woman infected with hantavirus after being repatriated from the MV Hondius cruise ship has the "most severe form of cardiopulmonary presentation," a doctor at the Paris hospital treating her said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Children in Gaza find relief from war trauma through breakdancing
video:video.video:description: Breakdance is helping Palestinian children in Gaza cope with the trauma of war. In the Nuseirat refugee camp, boys and girls from different ages gather every week to dance among the rubble, finding moments of relief and support by dancing.

video:video.video:title: Denver families dust off winter gear for spring snowstorm
video:video.video:description: A late spring snowstorm eased across parts of Colorado after closing schools, delaying flights and turning roads slushy on Wednesday. (AP Video by Thomas Peipert)

video:video.video:title: Trump says Marty Makary is out as Food and Drug Administration head
video:video.video:description: The head of the Food and Drug Administration, Dr. Marty Makary, is resigning after a rocky tenure that drew months of complaints from health industry executives, anti-abortion activists, vaping lobbyists and other allies of President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: US-China summit ends with Xi and Trump claiming progress but differences remain
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday, flying back to Washington after a a private meeting at Chinese leader Xi Jinping's official residence.

video:video.video:title: Gunshots heard in the Philippine Senate as authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator, who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Princess Catherine visits Italy on first solo trip abroad after cancer goes into remission
video:video.video:description: Britain’s Princess Catherine made her first overseas trip since announcing her cancer was in remission, traveling to Italy on Wednesday for a two-day tour focused on an early childhood educational approach that was developed here and exported globally.

video:video.video:title: Rio favela brings back street art tradition for World Cup
video:video.video:description: World Cup celebrations are already underway in Rocinha, Rio de Janeiro’s largest favela, on Tuesday, as local artists and residents painted an important commercial street.

video:video.video:title: New US postage stamps capture essence of historic Route 66
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Postal Service is releasing a new series of stamps to honor Route 66’s centennial. The offering includes eight stamps — one each for the states traversed by the Mother Road.

video:video.video:title: Banksy confirms central London statue of man blinded by flag is his
video:video.video:description: A new sculpture in central London of a man striding off a plinth with his face blinded by a billowing flag was claimed Thursday by the elusive street artist Banksy, more than a day after it mysteriously appeared.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Professional mourners mix tribal tradition with Kenya’s widespread Christianity
video:video.video:description: Loud and expressive mourners are common at funerals around the world—but in parts of Kenya, some of them may be paid workers.

video:video.video:title: Long Island Rail Road shuts down as workers strike
video:video.video:description: A marathon day of negotiations that finished early Monday failed to end a strike that shut down the Long Island Rail Road, North America’s largest commuter rail system, setting the stage for a rough start to the work week for about 250,000 commuters.

video:video.video:title: Naomi Osaka leaves hotel for Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Naomi Osaka stunned as she left The Mark Hotel for the Met Gala in a dramatic Robert Wun white sculptural fitted dress with exaggerated shoulders and adorned with red feathers and a matching headpiece.

video:video.video:title: A mosque and two vehicles are vandalized in the occupied West Bank as settler's violence intensifies
video:video.video:description: A mosque and two vehicles were vandalized in the occupied West Bank village of Jibiya early Friday, according to residents and the Palestinian Ministry of Religious Affairs, as settler's violence intensifies in the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, May 14th: Trump and Xi Jinping meet in Beijing for talks; Jerusalem braces for march with ultranationalists; Yemen and Houthi rebels agree to detainee swap; Newly restored tombs in Luxor are inaugurated.

video:video.video:title: Nearby resident recounts San Diego mosque shooting
video:video.video:description: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at an Islamic Center in San Diego on Monday and killed three men before killing themselves a few blocks away, police said.

video:video.video:title: A citizen campaign returns iconic kiwi birds to New Zealand's capital after century-long absence
video:video.video:description: The kiwi, New Zealand’s sacred national bird, vanished from the hills around Wellington more than a century ago, but now the capital's residents are waging an improbable citizen campaign to return the endangered flightless birds to the city.

video:video.video:title: Zoo in New Orleans welcomes rare Hartmann’s mountain zebras for the first time
video:video.video:description: Hartmann’s mountain zebra are listed as a species vulnerable to extinction due to habitat loss from livestock farming, climate-induced drought, and competition for water.

video:video.video:title: Taiwanese wary ahead of Trump-Xi summit amid concerns over island’s future
video:video.video:description: As U.S. President Donald Trump prepares for a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping this week, many in Taiwan are watching nervously, uncertain whether the island’s future could become part of negotiations between Washington and Beijing.

video:video.video:title: King Charles visits Jewish area of London hit by antisemitic attacks
video:video.video:description: Britain's King Charles III visited Golders Green on Thursday, an area of London which has seen a series of attacks over the past few months that have sparked fear and anger in Britain's Jewish community.

video:video.video:title: Mick Jagger breaks down the meaning of 'Rough and Twisted'
video:video.video:description: As The Rolling Stones announce the July 10 release of new album “Foreign Tongues,” Mick Jagger explains "Rough and Twisted," first released under an alias in April.

video:video.video:title: Investigators searching home connected to killer of California college student Kristin Smart
video:video.video:description: Authorities entered the second day of their search Thursday at a home connected to the man convicted of killing 19-year-old college student Kristin Smart in 1996, according to law enforcement.

video:video.video:title: Timelapse video shows French aircraft carrier strike group transiting the Suez Canal
video:video.video:description: The nuclear-powered Charles de Gaulle is moving towards the Red Sea in preparation for a potential French-British mission in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Western states are increasingly using AI to spot wildfires
video:video.video:description: With a severe wildfire season forecast for the Western U.S., some communities are trying to get ahead of it using AI-monitored cameras.

video:video.video:title: Residents near Tenerife port feel divided ahead of arrival of hantavirus-hit cruise ship
video:video.video:description: Residents in Tenerife’s southern town of San Isidro voiced a mix of fear and sympathy Saturday as a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak prepared to arrive in the Canary Islands with more than 140 passengers and crew on board.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 9
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Saturday, May 9th: Frontier Airlines plane hits and kills pedestrian at Denver airport; Reports of fatalities in Lebanon after Israeli strikes; Hungary’s Péter Magyar sworn in as prime minister; 3,400-year-old ballgame survives in Mexico.

video:video.video:title: Man charged in White House correspondents' dinner attack pleads not guilty
video:video.video:description: A California man accused of storming the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner while armed with guns and knives has pleaded not guilty to charges he attempted to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Medical plane crashes and sparks fire in New Mexico mountain range
video:video.video:description: A small medical plane crashed in a mountain range outside Ruidoso, New Mexico, before dawn Thursday, killing all four people aboard and sparking a wildfire in the surrounding forest, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Boxing becomes an outlet for girls in Gaza
video:video.video:description: Under the blazing sun in Gaza’s Muwasi area of Khan Younis, girls of different ages line up between rows of tents to practice boxing, throwing punches at worn punching bags and makeshift training pads fashioned from plastic poles and padded tarps.

video:video.video:title: Spain's Sanchez accuses Israel and Netanyahu of 'illegal abduction' of Gaza flotilla activists
video:video.video:description: Israeli authorities said they were taking two high-profile activists who led an aid flotilla bound for Gaza, and who were captured by Israel in international waters of the Mediterranean Sea, to Israel for questioning.

video:video.video:title: Iran war, Taiwan and unresolved trade tensions all likely to feature on agenda for Trump-Xi meeting
video:video.video:description: Few expect major breakthroughs to the long-running frictions between China and the U.S., which range from competition in technology to the thorny question of Taiwan, whose main ally is the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Widow of FSU shooting victim is suing OpenAI, attorney calls ChatGPT a 'co-conspirator'
video:video.video:description: The lawsuit claims that Phoenix Ikner, the alleged gunman who was a 20-year-old FSU student at the time, used ChatGPT for "input and assistance" to carry-out the shooting that killed two people and wounded six others.

video:video.video:title: Spirit Airlines goes out of business after 34 years, ending operations immediately
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines, an impish upstart that shook the industry with its irreverent ads and deep discount fares, announced Saturday that it has gone out of business after 34 years.

video:video.video:title: Assault trial of former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs begins with contrasting accounts
video:video.video:description: Former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs’ former private chef testifies in his assault trial.

video:video.video:title: Judge unseals purported Jeffrey Epstein suicide note
video:video.video:description: A note that Jeffrey Epstein 's former cellmate claims to have found after the financier's first suspected jail suicide attempt in 2019 has been made public.

video:video.video:title: Leaders gather in Yerevan for European Political Community meeting as Ukraine and Iran wars loom
video:video.video:description: The summit will be co-chaired by European Council President António Costa and Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan.

video:video.video:title: Events mark Missing and Murdered Indigenous Peoples awareness day
video:video.video:description: Across the country, Indigenous people are gathering this week to honor loved ones who are missing or have been killed and to call for better data collection, law enforcement response and reforms to make their communities safer.

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'I don't think about Americans' financial' woes during Iran talks
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he does not consider Americans’ financial strain “even a little bit” as he works to end the Iran conflict, comments that came as new data showed another sharp rise in consumer prices, driven in part by higher gas costs tied to the war.

video:video.video:title: Are fish and chips under threat in the UK?
video:video.video:description: For years, Brockley’s Rock in southeast London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang eats noodles on the streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus outbreak: 3 patients evacuated from cruise ship, new case confirmed in Switzerland
video:video.video:description: South African health authorities have identified the Andes strain of hantavirus in two passengers who were on a cruise ship at the center of an outbreak of the rare infection.

video:video.video:title: Police: Two suspects kill 3 people at a San Diego mosque before killing themselves
video:video.video:description: Authorities say two teenage suspects opened fire at an Islamic center in San Diego, California, killing three men before taking their own lives.

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores access to abortion pill mifepristone through telehealth, opponents disagree
video:video.video:description: A supporter of the ban points to FDA "safeguards" in place when the drug was approved. (AP video: Joseph B. Frederick)

video:video.video:title: Cannes and the Hôtel Martinez await a 'White Lotus' close-up
video:video.video:description: A look at The Hôtel Martinez, one of the iconic luxury hotels that line the beach in Cannes, which will be a central location for Season 4 of hit TV series, "The White Lotus."

video:video.video:title: Fashion expert weighs in on 2026 Met Gala looks and theme
video:video.video:description: Fashion writer Leigh Nordstrom of Women's Wear Daily highlights the most memorable looks and trends at the 2026 Met Gala including Beyoncé, Kim Kardashian and Eileen Gu.

video:video.video:title: Village festival combines catching fish with clearing weeds and silt
video:video.video:description: Villagers wade through a spring covered with algae, clearing the weeds. It's a century old tradition in the Himalayan region of Indian-controlled Kashmir that combines environmental conservation with a community celebration.

video:video.video:title: Thailand's controversial former leader Thaksin Shinawatra released after eight months in prison
video:video.video:description: Thailand’s former Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra, whose 21st-century political odyssey sharply divided Thai society for decades, was released from a Bangkok prison on Monday after serving eight months of a one-year sentence for a corruption-related charge.

video:video.video:title: Hazen Fire burns more than 900 acres in rural Arizona
video:video.video:description: Firefighters battle the Hazen Fire burning Near Buckeye, Arizona; the fire sparked on Saturday afternoon is estimated at 980 acres

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court weakens the Voting Rights Act, hollowing out law that protects minority voting rights
video:video.video:description: Six decades ago, the Voting Rights Act became one of the most consequential laws in the nation’s history. On Wednesday, the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Oakland high school that produced black MLB greats invests in new players
video:video.video:description: The Oakland high school that produced Black MLB greats Frank Robinson, Curt Flood and Vada Pinson is determined to keep baseball alive — and its new head coach Jamey Smart is ready to help.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore: AI can never replace true art
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival jury member Demi Moore gives her thoughts on the rise of AI in films.

video:video.video:title: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator
video:video.video:description: A burst of gunfire rang out Wednesday night in the Philippine Senate, where authorities have tried to arrest a senator who is wanted by the International Criminal Court for a charge of crime against humanity, an Associated Press journalist and other witnesses said.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge orders Louisiana to allow New Orleans exoneree to assume elected clerk office
video:video.video:description: A Louisiana exoneree who won election to a New Orleans clerk seat began work Monday after a federal judge temporarily blocked the state from eliminating the position.

video:video.video:title: May Day protesters block roadway at SFO's international terminal
video:video.video:description: Protesters blocked a road outside the international terminal at San Francisco’s airport on Friday, leading to the street's closure for about two hours.

video:video.video:title: Doctor identified among Pompeii victims thanks to analysis of the case he was carrying
video:video.video:description: One of the victims of the eruption that buried Pompeii in 79 AD. has been identified as a doctor thanks to analysis carried out on a small case that had remained hidden on them.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 13th: Trump in China to meet with Xi in Beijing; Prosecutors to retry Murdaugh after overturned murder convictions; Gunfire breaks out in the Philippine Senate; Tech improving the quality of life for dementia patients.

video:video.video:title: Aircraft carrier USS Gerald R. Ford returns home after 11-month deployment
video:video.video:description: The USS Gerald R. Ford returned home to Virginia on Saturday after an 11-month deployment, the longest since the Vietnam War.

video:video.video:title: Trump plans dramatic makeover for Eisenhower office building
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's proposal to put a coat of white paint on the exterior of a 19th-century historic landmark building next to the White House is slated for a hearing Thursday by a key federal agency he expects to approve what would be a dramatic makeover.

video:video.video:title: Nicolas Cage channels pop art, classic Hollywood in ‘Spider-Noir’
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Cage said he was inspired by pop artist Roy Lichtenstein and classic Hollywood actors as he developed “Spider-Noir.” (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives in Beijing to meet Xi as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: US President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Steve Cohen ending campaign in Tennessee after redraw of his Memphis district
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Steven Cohen of Tennessee ends his bid for reelection, his career upended by the redistricting battles that are sweeping the country after last month’s Supreme Court decision.

video:video.video:title: Trump hosts UFC fighters ahead of White House event in June
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump summoned reporters to the Oval Office on Wednesday touting the upcoming Ultimate Fighting Championship match that will take place at the White House in June.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Two newly-restored tombs inaugurated in the West Bank of Luxor
video:video.video:description: Ancient wall paintings and inscriptions inside the Luxor tombs have been restored after years of conservation work.

video:video.video:title: Reaction in Taiwan to Trump's remarks on arms sales as bargaining chip for negotiations with China
video:video.video:description: There has been a mixed reaction in Taiwan to U.S. President Donald Trump's remarks on arms sales to the self-ruled island after hearing concerns about it from China's leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Eurovision 2026 winner Dara describes what her song 'Bangaranga' means
video:video.video:description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever Eurovision victory.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll 'be making decisions' on Taiwan arms package after Xi summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he has not yet decided whether a major sale of US arms to Taiwan can move forward following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: FBI Director Kash Patel denies drinking allegations in heated Senate exchange
video:video.video:description: FBI Director Kash Patel angrily lashed out at a Democratic lawmaker during a budget hearing Tuesday, calling allegations that he drinks excessively on the job and has been unreachable at times to his staff “unequivocally, categorically false.”

video:video.video:title: Learn the Eurovision dance moves
video:video.video:description: Eurovision 2026 contestants contestants teach the dance routines for their songs.

video:video.video:title: USPS considers allowing people to ship handguns through the mail
video:video.video:description: Handguns could soon be mailed through the United States Postal Service for the first time in nearly 100 years if a proposed rule under the Trump administration takes effect.

video:video.video:title: Red carpet rolls out for 79th Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: The red carpet is out, ready to welcome stars to the 79th Cannes Film Festival which kicks off today.

video:video.video:title: Virologist explains what to know about hantavirus
video:video.video:description: Giulia Gallo, Postdoctoral Scientist in the Viral Glycoproteins Group at The Pirbright Institute told The Associated Press that "the most important thing is not to create panic" as the transmission rate for the virus is very low.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart stays fearless in the face of his own roast
video:video.video:description: Comedy superstar Kevin Hart tells his roasters - including Deon Cole, Mo Amer, Tom Segura - to "have at it," as they arrive for the live taping of Netlfix's "The Roast of Kevin Hart."

video:video.video:title: Beef consumption in Argentina falls to lowest level in 20 years
video:video.video:description: Red meat consumption in Argentina — historically one of the world’s biggest consumers of beef — has fallen to its lowest level in two decades. (AP/ Cristian Kovadloff)

video:video.video:title: Amputee footballers in Rwanda find healing and a sense of community
video:video.video:description: Amputee football offers a sense of community and healing in Rwanda, a country that went through its darkest time in 1994 during its genocide.

video:video.video:title: Body camera video shows moment house explodes as NYPD responds to call
video:video.video:description: A massive, fiery explosion at a house in Queens sent several New York City police officers flying through the air early Thursday morning as authorities were responding to a call of a man inside armed with a knife and the smell of gas.

video:video.video:title: Wounded veterans snorkel with a whale shark at the Georgia Aquarium
video:video.video:description: The Georgia Aquarium invited injured veterans from the Wounded Warrior Project to snorkel with a whale shark and manta rays as part of their Military Salute Program.

video:video.video:title: May Day rally in New York targets Amazon
video:video.video:description: May Day demonstrators in New York speak out against Amazon, which they say has been pushing small business out of communities.

video:video.video:title: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship arrive in Nebraska and Georgia
video:video.video:description: American passengers from virus-stricken cruise ship MV Hondius arrived in Nebraska and Georgia, where they will receive care and monitoring at medical facilities.
video:video.video:tag: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services

video:video.video:title: Traditional welcome for Taiwan president as he visits Eswatini king
video:video.video:description: Taiwan president Lai Ching-te is welcomed by Eswatini King Mswati III with a military ceremony at Lozitha Palace where they take part in a signing ceremony, bolstering ties with one of Taiwan's few remaining diplomatic allies despite growing international pressure from China.

video:video.video:title: Drone footage shows cruise ship at the center of a rare hantavirus outbreak waiting off Cape Verde
video:video.video:description: Three passengers have died and at least four people have been sick on board the MV Hondius, a Dutch cruise ship, in what health officials say is an outbreak of hantavirus, which usually spreads by inhaling contaminated rodent droppings.

video:video.video:title: 2 people found dead inside a plane that crashed into an Ohio home
video:video.video:description: Fire officials say two people were found dead in a plane after it crashed into a house in Ohio and exploded into flames.

video:video.video:title: Flames and violins fuse in Finland's Eurovision performance
video:video.video:description: Finnish duo of singer Pete Parkkonen and violinist Linda Lampenius bring the heat to Eurovision in an act that involves jets of flame, a valuable 18th-century violin and a team of “ninjas” working to avert disaster.

video:video.video:title: Philippine Senator at hearing after summons as part of probe into alleged extrajudicial killings
video:video.video:description: The International Criminal Court has unsealed an arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator linked to the deadly “war on drugs” overseen by ex-president Rodrigo Duterte, which allegedly involved the extrajudicial killings of suspects.

video:video.video:title: BTS members greet thousands of fans from balcony of Mexico's National Palace
video:video.video:description: BTS members greet thousands of fans from balcony of Mexico's National Palace

video:video.video:title: White House official says there are 'no cases of Ebola in America'
video:video.video:description: At a White House event about prescription drugs with President Donald Trump, Heidi Overton, a medical doctor who is deputy director of the White House Domestic Policy Council, was on hand to address any questions from reporters about the Ebola infections.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Views from inside the cruise ship after suspected hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship with nearly 150 people aboard was waiting for help off the coast of Cape Verde in the Atlantic Ocean on Monday after three passengers died and at least three other people were left seriously ill in a suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus, according to the World Health Organization

video:video.video:title: Indigenous leaders say the US-Mexico border wall is ruining sacred sites
video:video.video:description: Indigenous leaders say U.S. government contractors in the rush to expand the wall are desecrating Native American sacred places and cultural sites at an unprecedented pace.

video:video.video:title: Ultra-nationalist Jews arrive in Jerusalem's Old City for annual march
video:video.video:description: Jerusalem braced for tens of thousands of ultranationalist Jews to parade through Palestinian areas of the Old City on Thursday during an annual march that is often characterized by racist chants and violence.

video:video.video:title: 'Off Campus' provides a new, steamy, hockey romance
video:video.video:description: Smash hit drama "Heated Rivalry" doesn't return until 2027 but Prime Video's new series "Off Campus," also about hunky hockey players, is now streaming. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Drug counselor who delivered ketamine to Matthew Perry gets 2 years
video:video.video:description: Erik Fleming, the licensed drug addiction counselor who delivered “Friends” star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him, has been sentenced to two years in prison.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he is reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said on Saturday that he was reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records will be handed over to prosecutors in Florida DUI case
video:video.video:description: Prosecutors agreed to the defense's request for a protective order limiting the release of records only to prosecutors, law enforcement officers, state experts and Woods' defense team.

video:video.video:title: Amazon's deforestation drop meets a tougher foe: forest degradation racing ahead
video:video.video:description: Brazil is on track to record its lowest Amazon deforestation rates since 2012. Despite this, experts warn that forest degradation, driven by wildfires, logging, and drought, continues to outpace deforestation.

video:video.video:title: Fans gather in Vienna's Volksoper to watch final of Eurovision Song Contest
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest fans attended a screening of the grand final at the Volksoper, one of Vienna's opera houses. (May 16)

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga: 'You can rewrite your history'
video:video.video:description: Lady Gaga delivers a speech before a screening of “Apple Music Live: Lady Gaga Mayhem Requiem,” a filmed performance capturing her one-night-only show reimagining tracks from her “Mayhem” album.

video:video.video:title: Current and former Mississippi lawmakers prepare for a new chapter in the fight for voting rights
video:video.video:description: Former Mississippi State Rep. Edward Blackmon Jr. and his son, State Sen. Bradford Blackmon, reflect on their generational fight for voting rights after a recent U.S.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Finland's contestants explain why they needed to play violin live at Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Finland got special permission for Linda Lampenius to play violin live at Eurovision for her performance with Pete Parkkonen.

video:video.video:title: Police in Istanbul detain hundreds trying to get to Taksim Square in May Day Demo
video:video.video:description: Police detained at least 15 protesters who were trying to reach Istanbul's Taksim Square Friday, the epicenter of landmark 2013 protests, in defiance of a government ban.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors release video of armed man storming correspondents’ dinner
video:video.video:description: Federal prosecutors released a video Thursday showing the moment authorities said a man armed with guns and knives tried to storm the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner and attempt to kill President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Lionel Richie on 'the power of dreaming'
video:video.video:description: Lionel Richie reflects on his musical legacy: "That's the power of dreaming."

video:video.video:title: Maine lumber mill explosion injures at least 11 as firefighters respond to roaring blaze
video:video.video:description: A large fire and explosion at a lumber mill in Maine's midcoast region injured at least 11 people Friday.

video:video.video:title: Russia is ramping up its attempts to kill opponents in Europe, intelligence officials say
video:video.video:description: While Russian officials have long been accused of silencing the country’s enemies abroad, three Western intelligence officials from different countries told AP that a campaign of targeted killings has ramped up since President Vladimir Putin’s 2022 invasion of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Former Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs arrives in court for assault trial
video:video.video:description: Jury selection begins in the assault trial of former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs. Diggs pleaded not guilty in February to charges stemming from an alleged dispute with his former private chef.

video:video.video:title: Inside the Rolling Stones’ album event
video:video.video:description: Mick Jagger, Ronnie Wood and Keith Richards talk about upcoming Rolling Stones album, "Foreign Tongues," before a New Yok audience including Leonardo DiCaprio.

video:video.video:title: The lighting technology that brings the Eurovision Song Contest staging alive
video:video.video:description: The countdown is underway to the Eurovision Song Contest grand final. The 70th edition of the event, being held this year in Vienna, is one of the most technically ambitious ever.

video:video.video:title: Fast-growing brush fire in Southern California threatens homes
video:video.video:description: The fire was reported Monday just after 10 a.m. in Simi Valley, a city in Ventura County northwest of Los Angeles.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 15
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 15th: Trump says he's undecided on sending weapons to Taiwan; Iran's FM says lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the U.S.; Pentagon withdraws U.S. troops from Europe; Judge declares mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's rape retrial.

video:video.video:title: Is the UK's traditional fish and chip supper under threat?
video:video.video:description: Britain’s traditional fish and chip supper is under pressure, from rising fish prices and tighter catch limits to the knock on effects of global conflict and higher energy costs.

video:video.video:title: Former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs arrives in court
video:video.video:description: A woman who worked as a live-in chef for Stefon Diggs and is accusing the former New England Patriots wide receiver of assault is expected to continue testimony at his trial, which is marked by sharply conflicting accounts of what happened inside his home.

video:video.video:description: Florida Power & Light Company's mock hurricane drill simulates a response to a Category 2 hurricane named Aiden impacting the Vero Beach, Florida area.
