video:video.video:description: Residents in Tehran reacted on Tuesday to comments by U.S President Donald Trump about the ceasefire between Iran and the U.S. (AP video by: Saeed Sarmadi)

video:video.video:title: Hundreds attend anti-Israeli government rally in Tel Aviv
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Israeli demonstrators attended an anti-government rally in Tel Aviv on Saturday evening.

video:video.video:title: Witness describes scene after man wildly shoots a rifle at drivers near Boston
video:video.video:description: A witness whose apartment overlooks the scene initially thought he was hearing construction equipment when the banging noises started.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Bulgaria capital are pleased following Dara's Eurovision victory and grand welcome home
video:video.video:description: Residents of Sofia reacted with excitement after Bulgarian singer, Dara, won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 12
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, May 12th: Trump rejects Iran’s latest proposal to end war; Trump proposes suspending federal gas tax; Alabama could eliminate largely Black Congressional district; White House Correspondents Dinner suspect pleads not guilty.

video:video.video:title: Rubio meets Pope Leo on mission to ease tensions after Trump's criticism over Iran
video:video.video:description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Thursday. His visit was expected to be a fence-mending mission after weeks of tensions over the war in Iran in which US President Donald Trump has repeatedly bashed Pope Leo over his promotion of peace in the region.

video:video.video:title: Charli xcx poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Charli xcx poses on the 2026 Met Gala carpet

video:video.video:title: Iran war adds tension to Trump-Xi summit in China
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is heading back to China, but this time, the Iran war raises the stakes for both sides. China is the bigger purchaser of Iranian oil.

video:video.video:title: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara after historic victory
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of fans waving Bulgarian flags gathered at Sofia Airport as the singer arrived carrying the Eurovision trophy after winning with her song “Bangaranga.”

video:video.video:title: Supreme Court restores telehealth access to abortion pill mifepristone
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court on Monday restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: CNN founder Ted Turner, a pioneer of cable TV news, dies at 87
video:video.video:description: Ted Turner, a brash television pioneer who raced yachts, owned huge chunks of the American West and transformed the news business by launching CNN and introducing the 24-hour cable news cycle, died Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Deon Cole reflects on Altadena benefit show
video:video.video:description: Deon Cole reflects on hosting a series of Netflix-sponsored stand-up comedy shows in Altadena as the community rebuilds after last year's deadly Eaton Fire.

video:video.video:title: Man accused of attacking OpenAI CEO's home pleads not guilty to attempted murder
video:video.video:description: The man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the San Francisco home of OpenAI CEO Sam Altman pleaded not guilty to charges of attempted murder and attempted arson.

video:video.video:description: It was giddy up and go in Frankfurt on Saturday as the country's hobby horsers took part in their second ever championships.

video:video.video:description: Workers on New York’s Long Island Rail Road are on strike, paralyzing the busiest commuter rail system in North America.

video:video.video:title: Israel and Lebanon agree to 45-day extension of ceasefire, US says
video:video.video:description: US officials says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to 45-day extension of ceasefire. It comes after two “productive” days of talks in Washington and will be followed by more negotiations June 2-3, said State Department spokesman.

video:video.video:description: Star Wars' Grogu is ready to take over the big screen in "The Mandalorian and Grogu."

video:video.video:description: Letter writing, like vinyls and analogue photography, is making a come back. In 2021, the U.S. Postal Service sent cards and envelopes to 25,000 elementary school classrooms for a pen pal project, but older students also are putting pen to paper

video:video.video:description: The mountain bongo is being reintroduced into the wild by conservationists to increase the number of the rare antelope that are indigenous to Kenya’s forests.

video:video.video:description: Police say a man shot five people in Texas on Tuesday, killing two of them, at a shopping center north of Dallas in an area known as Koreatown in Carrollton.

video:video.video:description: King Charles III and Queen Camilla visited London’s renowned Chelsea Flower Show Monday.

video:video.video:description: The Venice Biennale has begun previewing its 61st edition, just days after the contemporary art show's jury resigned over the participation of Israel and Russia.

video:video.video:description: Southeast Asian leaders expressed "serious concern" over the war in Iran and called for a "cessation of hostilities" as they met in Philippines on Friday for an annual summit.

video:video.video:description: Bulgarian singer Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest on Saturday with her infectious party anthem “Bangaranga,” giving the southeast European country its first-ever Eurovision victory.

video:video.video:description: The Iraqi military took journalists to the site of a reported Israeli outpost in the desert on Sunday to show there was no indication of a long-term military presence at the site.

video:video.video:description: At least five people were killed and 12 others injured after a Russian airstrike on Kramatorsk on Tuesday, local authorities reported.

video:video.video:title: Cate Blanchett: small acts are the key to freedom
video:video.video:description: Cate Blanchett calls for engagement in society and the rejection of fear-based narratives, as her Displacement Film Fund announces its 2026 cohort of recipients, in Cannes.

video:video.video:description: Swedish, American and Ukrainian forces are taking part in Swedish-led military exercises on the Baltic island of Gotland.

video:video.video:title: Mýa releases first album in 8 years
video:video.video:description: Mya reveals which artists she "salutes" on "Retrospect," her first album in eight years.

video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization Director-General on Monday thanked Spanish authorities for safely evacuating passengers from a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak.

video:video.video:description: Firefighters were attempting to contain a large fire in the forest of the Chernobyl exclusion zone on Friday, Ukrainian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Bulgaria celebrates historic first Eurovision victory as Dara wins with “Bangaranga”
video:video.video:description: Bulgarians celebrated and cheered their first-ever victory at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest with singer Dara’s party anthem “Bangaranga.”

video:video.video:description: One person was killed in the latest attack on Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia on Friday, according to State Emergency Service of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: The UK’s last silk ribbon weavers keep their craft alive
video:video.video:description: Silk ribbon weaving was once a major UK industry, back when ribbons were a symbol of status and going out to buy them was considered a popular pastime for Georgian and Victorian women.

video:video.video:title: France reckons with Nazi-looted art in new Paris museum gallery
video:video.video:description: Last month, the museum launched its first research unit dedicated to tracing the orphans’ rightful heirs, file by file.

video:video.video:title: Karachi Port emerges as key regional transshipment hub as Strait of Hormuz remains closed

video:video.video:description: On a beachside road near Nuseirat in central Gaza, which is roughly the length of a marathon, two thousand people ran a 5K race, while 15 disabled people, including amputees, completed a 2K on Friday.

video:video.video:title: South Korean startup puts cameras on workers to teach AI robots
video:video.video:description: RLWRLD is among a wave of South Korean high-tech firms and manufacturers competing in the unproven yet fiercely contested global market for “physical AI.”

video:video.video:title: NVIDIA chief Jensen Huang eats noodles on streets of Beijing
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang was spotted on Friday trying some of Beijing’s delicacies. He was seen at No. 69 Fangzhuanchang Noodles, tucking into a bowl of “zhajiangmian” — a Beijing specialty that features noodles covered in a thick soybean-paste sauce mixed with vegetables and meat.

video:video.video:title: Iran war disruptions spark higher costs and lost income in Bangladesh
video:video.video:description: Tariqul Islam lost his savings after setbacks in his clothing business about a year and a half ago and turned to ride-sharing on his motorbike to make ends meet.

video:video.video:title: The humanitarian situation in Colombia has reached its worst level in a decade, says the International Committee of the Red Cross
video:video.video:description: The impact of armed conflict on civilians in Colombia over the past year has been the worst in a decade as the country's security situation deteriorates, the International Committee of the Red Cross said Tuesday in an annual report.

video:video.video:title: How a court case and loyal fans shaped Kneecap’s second record “FENIAN”
video:video.video:description: Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap were inspired by a legal battle while recording their second record “FENIAN,” even sampling the chants of support from their fans waiting outside of the court.

video:video.video:title: Trump's '51st US state' talk met with defiance in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Twice in as many days this week, U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed interest in turning Venezuela into his country’s 51st state.

video:video.video:title: What did Ian McKellen find when he Googled himself?
video:video.video:description: As she promotes new drama "The Christophers," British screen icon Sir Ian McKellen reveals the furry film role he first found when he Googled himself. (May 8)

video:video.video:title: Emmanuel Macron joins two-time Olympic champion Eliud Kipchoge for morning run in Nairobi
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron joined legendary Kenyan marathon runner Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning jog in Nairobi on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Lasers and robots: US farmers turning to automation amid labor shortages and aging workforce
video:video.video:description: As the U.S. agricultural workforce shrinks and ages, farmers are increasingly adopting artificial intelligence and robotic systems to manage everything from weed control to dairy production.

video:video.video:title: How do global events affect gas prices at the pump?
video:video.video:description: Gas prices move up and down in response to disruptions in supply and spikes in demand. Many global events such as conflicts, sanctions or decisions by major oil producing countries can limit or create uncertainty about the supply of oil.

video:video.video:title: President Trump returns to White House; says bilateral trip to China was a 'tremendous success'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called his three-day trip to China a 'tremendous success' after meeting President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, May 8th: UAE reports missile and drone attack; Trump says Iran ceasefire is holding; Man sentenced to life in prison for firebomb attack on demonstrators in Colorado; Tennessee Republicans enact new Congressional districts.

video:video.video:title: A musket not a firearm? Tell that to George Washington!
video:video.video:description: If you go to a reenactment this 250th anniversary year, you might be surprised to know that the muskets being fired aren't technically considered firearms in most states.

video:video.video:title: Wild animals are making their homes next to ours. Experts say it’s only natural
video:video.video:description: Spring is when wildlife nest or bear young, often close to humans. Experts say homeowners should enjoy their new neighbors but leave nests and young animals alone.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief says all passengers onboard cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak disembarked
video:video.video:description: The WHO said it has confirmed 11 cases, including three people from the cruise who died. All confirmed cases are among passengers or crew of the MV Hondius cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: Alex Murdaugh sues clerk whose behavior led state Supreme Court to overturn his murder convictions
video:video.video:description: The legal defense team for Alex Murdaugh announced a lawsuit against the court clerk whose behavior during his murder trial led the South Carolina Supreme Court to overturn his convictions and life sentence for the deaths of his wife and son.

video:video.video:title: Fire at a shopping center in Iran kills 8 people and injures 40
video:video.video:description: Tehran's emergency service department says a fire in a shopping center near Tehran has killed eight people and injured 40.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, Residents in southwest Mississippi reeling after tornadoes hit; Tennessee lawmakers pass redistricting legislation; Global health authorities scramble to contain hantavirus outbreak; Rubio meets with Pope Leo to mend relations.

video:video.video:title: Animal welfare group finds systemic neglect at public dog shelters in Romania
video:video.video:description: An undercover investigation into conditions in Romania’s publicly funded shelters for stray dogs, carried out by the international animal welfare organization Vier Pfoten, known as Four Paws, found what it describes as “high death rates and disturbing conditions” amounting to “systemic neglect.”

video:video.video:title: Passenger says atmosphere on board ship at centre of hantavirus outbreak is 'relatively good'
video:video.video:description: Helene Goessaert, a passenger of a cruise ship at the center of a rare-virus outbreak, says that the atmosphere on board the ship is 'relatively good'.

video:video.video:title: New wave of gang violence in Haiti displaces hundreds of people
video:video.video:description: A new wave of gang violence in Haiti’s capital forced hundreds to flee their homes over the weekend, leaving families scattered along the road to the country’s main airport on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo to spend first anniversary of his election as pope in Naples
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is spending the first anniversary of his election as Pope in Naples, where he'll pray at a sanctuary devoted to the Madonna.

video:video.video:title: Families scour southern Mexico after 40 migrants vanished on a dangerous sea route
video:video.video:description: Relatives of dozens of missing migrants travelled to Mexico’s southern Pacific coast to search for clues about family members who disappeared while attempting a maritime journey toward the United States.

video:video.video:title: Isabel Allende gives 'The House of the Spirits' TV adaptation her stamp of approval
video:video.video:description: Best-selling author Isabel Allende has given Prime Video's "The House of the Spirits" her stamp of approval.

video:video.video:title: WHO expert says Hantavirus outbreak 'is not the next COVID'
video:video.video:description: A top epidemic expert at the U.N. health agency said Wednesday that the hantavirus outbreak is not the next COVID pandemic.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of artists bring new life to gigantic former ironworks on UNESCO's world heritage list
video:video.video:description: 50 urban artists from 17 countries converge on one of Europe's most important industrial landmarks between May and November for a show that takes advantage of the former ironworks' sprawling spaces and aura of abandonment.

video:video.video:title: Foxhounds kick off horse races at the Iroqouis Steeplechase with howling fun dog parade
video:video.video:description: At the Iroquois Steeplechase, the horses are competing for the top purses, but the fan favorite is the parade of the hounds.

video:video.video:title: Access to abortion pills under fire
video:video.video:description: The Supreme Court this week restored broad access to the abortion pill mifepristone, blocking a lower-court ruling that had threatened to upend one of the main ways abortions are provided across the nation.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Parents mourn their son as a town in Argentina faces its own hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: Daisy Morinigo and David Delgado said they initially thought their 14-year-old son had the flu when he came down with a fever and body aches.

video:video.video:title: Thousands gather in London for rival protests as police separate opposing groups
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of protesters descend on London on Saturday for two major demonstrations.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is more than ready for the Eurovision stage
video:video.video:description: Australia is fielding "Born to Try" star Delta Goodrem as its entry for the 70th Eurovision Song Contest, where she will compete with "Eclipse." (May 11)

video:video.video:title: Suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship raises questions about source and spread
video:video.video:description: A rodent-borne illness is suspected of causing an outbreak aboard a cruise ship that has killed three people and sickened others.
video:video.video:tag: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

video:video.video:title: Cubans struggle to survive on pocket-sized government ration book as products dwindle
video:video.video:description: Cuba's pocket-sized government ration book has been circulating for more than six decades. It used to offer a bounty of products, but that offer has dwindled in recent years as the island's economic crisis deepened.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus-stricken cruise ship arrives at Tenerife in Spain's Canary Islands
video:video.video:description: The ship won't dock but will remain anchored, with people ferried off in small boats. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, and will only be taken off the ship once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations.

video:video.video:title: Connor Storrie arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Connor Storrie at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Shakira's free concert on Copacabana beach draws 2 million fans
video:video.video:description: The performance followed similar shows by Madonna in 2024 and Lady Gaga last year — also attended by huge crowds that danced on the sprawling sands.

video:video.video:title: Invalides monument in Paris: 350 years of care for wounded soldiers and war victims
video:video.video:description: World-famous as the resting place of Napoleon, the gilded dome of the Invalides in Paris has drawn millions of visitors, but behind the landmark’s grand façade was a lesser-known mission: serving as a home and hospital for wounded soldiers and victims of war for more than 350 years.

video:video.video:title: Two killed in Israeli strike on civilian vehicle in Gaza, health officials say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed and others wounded after an Israeli airstrike hit a vehicle in Gaza City on Saturday evening, according to local health officials.

video:video.video:title: Iran war energy shock raises Asia's interest in fuel blending
video:video.video:description: India is pushing for the use of more biofuels in transportation in order to slash dependence on imported fossil fuels, which have been disrupted by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Most active volcano in the Philippines spews giant ash column sky high
video:video.video:description: The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology said the alert level remained at 3, based on its latest 24-hour monitoring.

video:video.video:title: Three hantavirus patients evacuated from cruise ship off Cape Verde
video:video.video:description: Three patients were evacuated from a cruise ship off Cape Verde on Wednesday amid a hantavirus outbreak, as a new case was confirmed in Switzerland.

video:video.video:title: South Carolina redistricting proposal moves to Senate after House approval
video:video.video:description: The South Carolina House on Wednesday approved a resolution giving lawmakers permission to return later, after their regular work ends, to redraw congressional districts that could eliminate the state’s only Democratic-held district.

video:video.video:title: 'A moment of sacrifices': Italian farmers wrestle with economic fallout of Iran war
video:video.video:description: Since Israel and U.S. attacked Iran on Feb. 28, diesel for agricultural vehicles in Italy has risen from 0.85 euros ($1) per litre to nearly 1.50 ($1.76), while fertilizer ingredients have gone up by 35%.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of Russian strikes on Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles
video:video.video:description: Russia targeted Ukraine's capital with a mass drone and missile attack early Thursday morning that killed at least one and injured 33 people, local authorities said.

video:video.video:description: Michigan Fab Five member and Detroit high school founder Jalen Rose has his ‘Mama, I made it’ moment as the commencement speaker at the University of Michigan in Ann Arbor.

video:video.video:title: Your climate impact doesn't end when you die. More people are considering 'greener' death options
video:video.video:description: As interest in alternative forms of burial has risen in recent years, some have turned to green, or conservation burials, to reduce the environmental impact of their passing, such as at the Prairie Creek Conservation Cemetery outside Gainesville, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone pilots turn military exercise in Sweden into critical warning for NATO
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drone pilots turned a military exercise in Sweden into a critical warning for NATO. The pilots, playing the role of the enemy in the exercise, said they destroyed Swedish forces with drones — and that if the exercise had been a real life scenario, the Western forces would have been dead.

video:video.video:title: Walk in the shoes of a Met Gala attendee

video:video.video:title: How to decide when to get a mammogram

video:video.video:description: Farmers already stressed by the war at home between Sudan's military and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces are now watching costs rise due to war in the Middle East.

video:video.video:description: For more than a century, only conservators and experts could catch a close-up glimpse of early works by Austrian artist Gustav Klimt in Vienna.

video:video.video:description: Soaring costs for fuel due to the Iran war are leading panicked consumers in hard-hit Asia toward rooftop solar power, a likely windfall for China as the world’s largest provider of solar technology.

video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is arguing that the war in Iran has already ended because of the ceasefire that began in April, an interpretation that would allow the White House to avoid a federal law requirement for congressional approval of military action that extends beyond 60 days.

video:video.video:description: Authorities said four people were critically injured after a car struck pedestrians in the city of Modena, with one woman requiring the amputation of both legs.

video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump wrapped up his visit to Beijing Friday after a private meeting at China's leader Xi Jinping's official residence and departed for Washington.

video:video.video:title: Milly Alcock’s 'punk rock' Supergirl takes flight
video:video.video:description: Milly Alcock says she sees a lot of herself in Supergirl, and jokes about how surreal it is to have her own action figure. (May 2)

video:video.video:description: In only five days, nearly 1,000 volunteers with Habitat for Humanity will complete 24 affordable housing units in Atlanta's Sylvan Hills neighborhood.
