video:video.video:description: This photo gallery highlights some of the most compelling images from around the world, published by The Associated Press during the week of 9-14 May 2026

video:video.video:title: Man with assault rifle wildly shoots at drivers near Boston, wounding 2 before being shot
video:video.video:description: Video captured a man with an assault-style rifle fired randomly at passing cars while striding down a busy road outside Boston until he was wounded when a state trooper and a former Marine opened fire.

video:video.video:title: US says 'Project Freedom' will defend shipping in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: U.S. forces launched an effort to guide commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz, with the first two ships, both American-flagged merchant vessels, sailing through unscathed.

video:video.video:title: Protesters march at Tennessee's capitol as lawmakers look to carve up a majority-Black district in Memphis
video:video.video:description: Protesters marched and shouted "Shame!" at Tennessee Republicans who called a special legislative session that could potentially carve up a majority-Black district in Memphis.

video:video.video:title: Friends and family pay final respects to young woman killed in Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Maryna Homeniuk, an English teacher, and her boyfriend Yurii Orlov, a former hockey player, were killed in this week's Russian strikes on Kyiv.

video:video.video:title: Pentagon releases video after US fires on and disables 2 more Iranian tankers
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says its forces fired on and disabled two Iranian oil tankers Friday after exchanging fire with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz overnight.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Actress Anne Hathaway makes her debut at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Evidence points to human remains at home linked to Kristin Smart’s killing, sheriff says
video:video.video:description: Authorities in California say evidence suggests human remains were present at a home connected to the man convicted of killing Kristin Smart, a 19-year-old college student who went missing in 1996.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt with police as people in Manila march to the US Embassy on May Day
video:video.video:description: Workers in the Philippines clashed with police near the US Embassy in the capital Manila on Friday during their Labor Day protest. (AP video by Joeal Calupitan)

video:video.video:title: Cannes Film Festival rolls out iconic red carpet
video:video.video:description: The red carpet of the famous steps of Palais des Festivals is put in place, ready to welcome stars to the 79th Cannes Film Festival, in the South of France. (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberating in assault trial of former Patriots wide receiver Stefon Diggs
video:video.video:description: The defense and prosecution deliver closing arguments in the assault trial of former New England Patriots receiver Stefon Diggs after calling multiple witnesses to the stand

video:video.video:title: Trump calls Iran conflict a 'skirmish,' insists US has 'total control'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump downplayed tensions with Iran Tuesday, calling the conflict a “skirmish” and claiming the U.S. has “total control.”

video:video.video:title: Rubio arrives in Vatican ahead of audience with Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio opened a fence-mending visit to the Vatican on Thursday after President Donald Trump’s broadsides against Pope Leo XIV and the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Memorial stones mark former homes of Holocaust victims in Berlin
video:video.video:description: German artist Gunter Demnig has installed small Holocaust memorial stones in Berlin for 30 years. These plaques are called Stolpersteine or "stumbling blocks."

video:video.video:title: A rare sanctuary in Congo looks after baby bonobos away from poaching threat
video:video.video:description: A sanctuary on the outskirts of Congo's capital of Kinshasa is the world’s only sanctuary for orphaned bonobos, usually rescued from poachers.

video:video.video:title: Rare Franklin collection on display in Philadelphia before auction
video:video.video:description: The Library Company of Philadelphia — one of several institutions Franklin himself founded — is hosting selections from the Jay T.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 14
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 14TH: Chinese President Xi welcomes Trump to Beijing; Russia launches massive missile and drone attack on Kyiv, Ukraine; UAE denies that Israeli PM Netanyahu visited; Woman who wrote children’s book on grief sentenced for husband’s murder.

video:video.video:title: Severe weather leaves shipwrecks strewn across the Queensland coast
video:video.video:description: Dozens of vessels lie wrecked along the coastline of northern Australia after a season of severe summer weather, leaving state authorities to handle the cleanup.

video:video.video:title: 'I want that kind of jail to close'; French woman recalls her experience of US ICE detention centre
video:video.video:description: Marie-Therese Ross spoke to The Associated Press on Monday about her 16 harrowing days in federal immigration custody after being arrested on April 1 in Alabama following an alleged visa overstay, and the remarkable late-in-life love story that brought her to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Beloved former mayor in Kansas fears he might be deported over voting controversy
video:video.video:description: While seeking citizenship in 2025, Joe Ceballos, 55, who was brought to the U.S. from Mexico by family when he was 4, and is a legal permanent resident, admitted during an interview that he had voted, apparently not knowing his status does not allow it, his lawyer said.

video:video.video:title: Negotiators reach a deal to end strike on North America’s busiest commuter rail system
video:video.video:description: Negotiators have reached a deal to end a strike that has brought North America’s largest commuter rail system to a standstill.

video:video.video:title: Romania’s pro-European coalition collapses after no-confidence vote against prime minister
video:video.video:description: The joint effort was launched April 28 when the leftist Social Democratic Party, or PSD, which withdrew from the coalition in late April, and the hard-right opposition Alliance for the Unity of Romanians party, or AUR, submitted the motion to Parliament.

video:video.video:title: Judge allows gun and notebook as evidence at Mangione’s trial in UnitedHealthcare CEO’s killing
video:video.video:description: The judge allowed evidence obtained during a subsequent inventory of his backpack at the police station, but said evidence found during an initial search of Mangione’s backpack during his arrest at a McDonald’s restaurant must be suppressed.

video:video.video:title: Is the UK's traditional fish and chip supper under threat?
video:video.video:description: For over a decade Brockley’s Rock in south east London has been serving customers from its Lewisham shopfront, after opening in 2011, building on experience in the trade that dates back to 1989.

video:video.video:title: Shakira explains how her 2010 World Cup anthem 'Waka Waka' made her a mother
video:video.video:description: Shakira reminisces about her 2010 FIFA World Cup anthem "Waka Waka (This Time For Africa)," saying the song turned her into a mother. (May 15)

video:video.video:title: Bolivia protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital as unrest widens
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Surging gas prices, 52 percent higher than before th Iran war, raise costs of summer road trips
video:video.video:description: Rising gas prices are straining drivers' wallets, which could impact summer travel plans if the war in Iran drags on. (AP Video by Terry Chea)

video:video.video:title: USA should not be at Venice Biennale, says artist Anish Kapoor
video:video.video:description: A leading British artist has said the US should have been excluded from the Venice Biennale. Anish Kapoor made the comments amid controversy over the participation of Russia and Israel too.

video:video.video:title: Former LA police detective Mark Fuhrman, key figure in OJ Simpson murder trial, dies
video:video.video:description: Former Los Angeles police detective Mark Fuhrman, who was convicted of lying during testimony at the O.J. Simpson murder trial, has died. He was 74.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 11
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, May 11th: Americans evacuated from ship due to hantavirus flown to Nebraska; Trump rejects Iran’s latest response to U.S. proposal; Oil prices rise; Israel says it blew up tunnels in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 2 in the Caribbean
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military says it’s launched another strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea. The attack on Monday killed two people.

video:video.video:title: Demos held in Latin American countries to mark Labor Day
video:video.video:description: Demonstrators turned out in Venezuela, Cuba, Ecuador and Argentina. In Mexico City, sex workers held a protest demanding dignity and protections.

video:video.video:title: Leaders arrive at European Political Community summit in Armenia
video:video.video:description: European Union leaders and regional partners have arrived in Armenia to discuss regional and international issues at the 8th European Political Community summit.

video:video.video:title: Lady Gaga unleashes 'MAYHEM' in LA
video:video.video:description: On Thursday, Lady Gaga made a dramatic entrance at the premiere of “Lady Gaga: Apple Music Live (MAYHEM Requiem),” a special capturing her one-night-only Los Angeles performance earlier this year that reimagined songs from her Grammy-winning album “Mayhem.”

video:video.video:title: US military strike on alleged drug boat kills 3 in the eastern Pacific
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched another strike Tuesday on a vessel suspected of transporting drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing three men.

video:video.video:title: 'Pillion' star Harry Melling is ready to be transported back to Hogwarts
video:video.video:description: Harry Melling, who played Dudley Dursley in the original films, explains why he's looking forward to watching the new "Harry Potter" series. (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Trump celebrates the national champion Indiana Hoosiers at the White House
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump welcomed the 2026 national champion Indiana Hoosiers to the White House Monday, praising their undefeated 16-0 season.

video:video.video:title: How AP-NORC polls track presidential approval and key issues
video:video.video:description: Since its founding in 2011, The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research has produced hundreds of polls on timely issues, including the approval of U.S. presidents and their handling of the economy, immigration and foreign policy.

video:video.video:title: US Secretary of State comments on efforts over Iran war, relations with the Vatican
video:video.video:description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the US is expecting a response from Iran on Friday as diplomatic discussions continue.

video:video.video:title: Amy Grant reflects on resisting labels people put on her
video:video.video:description: In the nearly 50 years she's been working as a musician, Amy Grant has repeatedly resisted the labels others have sought to put on her.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he and Xi discussed Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he discussed Iran with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Trump said the two have similar feelings on Iran that they both don’t want Iran to have a nuclear weapon.

video:video.video:title: North America’s largest commuter rail system faces a potential shutdown
video:video.video:description: North America’s largest commuter rail system with 250,000 daily riders faces a shutdown if a deal isn't reached with unionized workers to avoid a strike.

video:video.video:title: A rich pour of heritage: Yemeni coffeehouses rise across the US
video:video.video:description: Yemeni coffeehouses are rapidly expanding across the U.S., blending centuries-old coffee traditions with modern café culture and serving as community-focused social spaces.

video:video.video:title: How it feels to be on the jury at Cannes
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Chloé Zhao and Stellan Skarsgård describe being invited onto the Cannes Film Festival jury, led by director Park Chan-wook.

video:video.video:title: South African police hoist crocodile from crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
video:video.video:description: A South African police officer was lowered alone into a "crocodile-infested river" in a daring effort to recover human remains.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish: David Attenborough is 'the GOAT'
video:video.video:description: Musician and climate campaigner Billie Eilish explains why she admires David Attenborough so much, as she promotes new concert movie "Billie Eilish – Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour" with iconic director James Cameron.

video:video.video:title: Fire at a shopping center in Iran kills several people and injures dozens
video:video.video:description: A fire in a shopping center near Iran’s capital killed several people and injured dozens, state TV said Wednesday. The fire started Tuesday

video:video.video:title: Xi hosts Trump for a welcoming ceremony in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts a welcoming ceremony for visiting U.S. President Donald Trump ahead of a bilateral meeting.

video:video.video:title: Quarantined cruise passenger gives Nebraska room tour, denies hantavirus infection
video:video.video:description: Jake Rosmarin of Boston is one of U.S. 15 passengers currently under observation in the National Quarantine Unit at the University of Nebraska Medical Center in Omaha after a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: Trump jokes that US Navy will take on Cuba on the way home from Iran
video:video.video:description: “Cuba’s got problems,” Trump said in his speech before the non-profit Forum Club of the Palm Beaches at the Raymond F. Kravis Center for the Performing Arts.

video:video.video:title: Xi says U.S.-China relationship is most important in the world
video:video.video:description: China’s leader Xi Jinping called for the United States and Beijing to work together as “partners” rather than "rivals" in an opening toast ahead of a state banquet that was largely positive, though measured.

video:video.video:title: Judge declares a mistrial in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial after jury deadlocks
video:video.video:description: Jurors deadlocked in Harvey Weinstein’s rape retrial Friday, forcing another mistrial in a #MeToo-era case that has gone to trial three times so far.

video:video.video:title: Miners use dynamite in protest demanding a labor reform
video:video.video:description: Clashes erupted Thursday in Bolivia's capital as police used tear gas to disperse a crowd of miners trying to breach the government palace and setting off small dynamite charges, a tactic that has become increasingly common during this second week of nationwide unrest.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan Taliban splinter group claims responsibility for deadly attack on security post
video:video.video:description: A suicide bomber and several gunmen detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near the post in Bannu, a district in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province bordering Afghanistan, late Saturday, police said.

video:video.video:title: Russia hits Kyiv with drones and ballistic missiles, injuring at least 4
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine's capital with a mass drone and missile attack in the early morning of Thursday, injuring at least four people, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Spanish Royal House releases video of Princess Leonor on an instruction flight
video:video.video:description: The Spanish Royal House released video on Tuesday of Princess Leonor de Borbon y Ortiz undertaking an instruction flight.

video:video.video:title: Trump set to arrive in Beijing as war and inflation weigh on his presidency
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to arrive in Beijing Wednesday for his highly anticipated summit with leader Xi Jinping at a restless moment for a world worried about war, trade and artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am. (May 7)

video:video.video:title: Hungary's incoming Prime Minister Péter Magyar prepares for his swearing in on Saturday
video:video.video:description: Péter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister on Saturday, ending Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule.

video:video.video:title: Casey Goodson Jr.'s family gets 'closure' following Jason Meade reckless homicide verdict
video:video.video:description: Community members and family of Casey Goodson Jr. react to ex-deputy Jason Meade being found guilty of reckless homicide in Goodson’s killing.

video:video.video:title: Protest groups block access to Russian pavilion at Venice Biennale
video:video.video:description: Russia’s participation in the first Biennale since the 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine led to Russian punk group Pussy Riot and members of Ukrainian feminist organization FEMEN rushing the exhibition area.

video:video.video:title: Damage at a mobile home park after powerful storms tear through several Mississippi counties
video:video.video:description: Authorities say powerful storms, that spawned at least three tornadoes, tore through several Mississippi counties, damaging nearly 500 homes and injuring at least 17 people.

video:video.video:title: 2 killed after Russian drone attack in Ukraine's southern Odesa region
video:video.video:description: The drones also hit port infrastructure, causing a fire that was later extinguished by emergency teams, the emergency service reported.

video:video.video:title: Decorated skeletons in Catholic churches in Bavaria take visitors by surprise
video:video.video:description: It is a sight that has sent shivers down the spines of many visitors: four complete skeletons draped in silk and brocade, adorned with precious stones, filigree gold, silver and lace that have been on display for centuries at the Catholic monastery church of Banz in southern Germany.

video:video.video:title: South Florida wildfires burn thousands of acres in the Everglades
video:video.video:description: A pair of South Florida wildfires that torched thousands of acres in the Everglades over the weekend spread Monday as fire crews worked to contain them.

video:video.video:title: A Vienna cafe offers a welcome for Israel supporters amid tensions at the Eurovision Song Contest
video:video.video:description: Vienna's famed coffeehouses are embracing the Eurovision Song Contest. They have also been touched by tensions over Israel’s inclusion in the sequin-drenched pop music extravaganza.

video:video.video:title: Beyoncé returns to Met Gala carpet after 10 years
video:video.video:description: Met Gala co-chair Beyoncé poses on the carpet for the first time in 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Iran and US at impasse and ceasefire shaky ahead of Trump's visit to China
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to use a trip this week to China to urge Chinese President Xi Jinping to pressure Iran into making concessions and end the limbo.

video:video.video:title: iPads in schools? AP follows fight against digital devices in a school district
video:video.video:description: Kids in Lower Merion school district in Pennsylvania get iPads starting in kindergarten, switch to Chromebooks in second grade and get their own MacBooks in eighth grade.

video:video.video:title: South Korean Buddhist sect welcomes humanoid robot
video:video.video:description: A humanoid robot in South Korea joined the Buddhist faith on Wednesday, during an ordination ceremony at Jogye Temple, located in central Seoul.

video:video.video:title: Oil prices sank, and stock markets burst higher worldwide
video:video.video:description: The price for a barrel of Brent fell below $102 Wednesday. On Wall Street, the S&P 500 climbed 1.5% and reached another record.

video:video.video:title: Trump says Iran wants to make a deal but he's 'not satisfied with it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he’s “not satisfied” with Iran’s latest proposal to end the war, citing “disjointed” leadership in Tehran.

video:video.video:title: Russia and Ukraine exchange prisoners, with each side releasing 205 detainees
video:video.video:description: Russia’s Defense Ministry said that Russia and Ukraine had a prisoner exchange on Thursday, each side releasing 205.

video:video.video:title: Russia launches new Soyuz-5 rocket in first test flight
video:video.video:description: A new Russian medium-class launch vehicle Soyuz-5 was launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome overnight Thursday as part of its first flight tests, Russia’s state space corporation Roscosmos said.

video:video.video:title: Returnees to southern Lebanon face difficult choices as they survey their flattened homes
video:video.video:description: Residents of southern Lebanon on Thursday surveyed damaged homes and buildings flattened to the ground.

video:video.video:title: AP catches up with fans watching Eurovision final at Vienna's Volksoper
video:video.video:description: AP spoke with fans attending a viewing of Eurovision's grand final at Vienna’s Volksoper.

video:video.video:title: Indonesia's Mount Dukono volcano eruption kills at least 3 hikers, 17 injured
video:video.video:description: Mount Dukono erupted in Indonesia’s North Maluku province, killing three hikers, including two Singaporean nationals and one Indonesian, authorities said Friday, as the volcano continued to spew ash high into the air and prompted renewed safety warnings.

video:video.video:title: Passengers evacuate after plane hits and kills person on Denver airport runway
video:video.video:description: Footage obtained by The Associated Press on Saturday showed passengers evacuating an aircraft by inflatable emergency slides following an accident at Denver International Airport.

video:video.video:title: Armed members of Iran's nomadic Bakhtiari tribe march in Tehran amid tensions with US
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of members of Iran's Bakhtiari tribe gathered in Tehran on Monday to pledge allegiance to Iran's supreme leader. (AP Video by Mohsen Ganji)

video:video.video:title: Chedrick Greene wins special election, ensuring Democrats keep state Senate control in Michigan
video:video.video:description: Democrat Chedrick Greene won a special election in Michigan, securing his party’s control of the state Senate through the remainder of Gov.

video:video.video:title: On day of Magyar swearing in, EU flag flies on Hungary parliament building for first time since 2014
video:video.video:description: The flag of the European Union was raised on the Hungary parliament building’s facade on Saturday afternoon for the first time since Viktor Orbán’s government removed it in 2014.

video:video.video:title: Magyar hails 'wonderful day' in address to crowd after inauguration as Hungary PM
video:video.video:description: Hungary's Péter Magyar addressed the crowd gathered in front of the parliament building where the country’s new national assembly was inaugurated, ending Viktor Orbán’s autocratic 16-year rule.

video:video.video:title: Billie Eilish says it's a "gift" to see her fans in live concert film
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish says it’s a "gift" to see her fans on screen during her concert movie "Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)"

video:video.video:title: Three Australian women back from Syria face slavery and terrorism charges for alleged IS group links
video:video.video:description: Three Australian women were charged on Friday with slavery and terrorism offenses after they arrived home from Syria with another 10 others whom police allege are linked to the Islamic State group.

video:video.video:title: Scientists develop an autonomous robot to map endangered coral reefs
video:video.video:description: Researchers in the United States have developed an autonomous underwater robot that can listen to coral reefs, track marine life and identify biodiversity hotspots.

video:video.video:title: Palestinian father-to-be killed during Israeli military raid as West Bank violence continues
video:video.video:description: Nayef Firas Ziad Samaro, 26, was killed, according to the Health Ministry, and his body was brought to the hospital where his wife was giving birth.

video:video.video:title: Zac Posen's Met Gala masterpiece
video:video.video:description: Here's how designer Zac Posen dressed Kendall Jenner as a Greek goddess at the Met Gala

video:video.video:title: What to know about hantavirus, the illness suspected in a cruise ship outbreak
video:video.video:description: Three people have died and others have been sickened by what health authorities suspect is a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean.

video:video.video:title: 'Good things are gonna happen:' Trump downplays differences over Iran as he heads to China summit
video:video.video:description: Trump said trade will be the focus, not the war, and predicted “good things” to come from the trip.

video:video.video:title: Ship with aid from Mexico and Uruguay sails into Havana port
video:video.video:description: A ship carrying humanitarian aid from Mexico and Uruguay arrived in Havana Monday as Cuba faces worsening shortages of food, fuel and electricity.

video:video.video:title: Imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi's husband voices family anguish over health fears
video:video.video:description: The health of imprisoned Iranian rights lawyer Narges Mohammadi was at “very high risk," her foundation and family said Saturday, adding that Iran's Intelligence Ministry was opposing her transfer to Tehran for treatment by her own doctors.

video:video.video:title: US and Bahrain call on Security Council to pass resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The draft resolution demands Iran halt attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz, stop imposing tolls, and disclose the placement of all mines to allow freedom of navigation.

video:video.video:title: Stars celebrate women in cinema at annual gala
video:video.video:description: Demi Moore, Diane Kruger and Rami Malek are a few of the stars who attended the Red Sea Film Foundation's 2026 "Women In Cinema" Gala. (May 14)

video:video.video:title: President Macron tells off noisy audience during presentation at Nairobi university event
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron gave a telling off to a noisy audience during a presentation at the University of Nairobi.

video:video.video:title: How Billie Eilish breathes new life into old songs
video:video.video:description: Billie Eilish describes her evolving relationship with her older songs, as she joins her co-director James Cameron at the Los Angeles launch of "Billie Eilish — Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D)" (May 7)

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, May 15th: U.S. and Nigerian forces conduct operation targeting Islamic State; Israeli strike kills Hamas military leader; Long Island Rail Road workers go on strike; Humanoid robot monks walk in South Korea parade ahead of Buddha's birthday.

video:video.video:title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
video:video.video:description: A giant new species of dinosaur discovered in Thailand is thought to be the biggest ever found in Southeast Asia.

video:video.video:title: Faith leaders in Ohio call on state legislators to repeal state's death penalty
video:video.video:description: A group of faith leaders representing hundreds of clergy members from across Ohio delivered a letter to state lawmakers urging them to repeal the state’s death penalty.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart shares 'message to all'
video:video.video:description: Kevin Hart shares a "message to all" at his live Netflix roast that featured stars like Tom Brady, Usher, Chelsea Handler and more.

video:video.video:title: UN chief pays tribute to television pioneer Ted Turner, who has died at age 87
video:video.video:description: Ted Turner, a brash television pioneer who raced yachts, owned huge chunks of the American West and transformed the news business by launching CNN and introducing the 24-hour cable news cycle, died Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Mother's Day traditions stay strong as gas prices rise, but summer vacations are another story
video:video.video:description: Gas prices have risen across the Silver State, as it has across the country, but moms say their Mother's Day plans have gone unchanged.

video:video.video:title: Hegseth says 'ceasefire is not over' despite Iranian strikes
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth says the shaky U.S.-Iran ceasefire remains intact despite recent Iranian strikes.

video:video.video:title: South African police officer hoists crocodile from crocodile-infested river to recover human remains
video:video.video:description: A South African police captain who hoisted a crocodile out of a river in a daring effort to recover human remains has been praised for his "extraordinary bravery".

video:video.video:title: Drone strike sparks fire at UAE nuclear power plant in latest blow to Iran ceasefire
video:video.video:description: A drone strike targeted the United Arab Emirates’ sole nuclear power plant on Sunday, sparking a fire on its perimeter.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III lays out UK government agenda as Starmer's job hangs in the balance
video:video.video:description: In a speech to lawmakers laced with all the pomp that accompanies the ceremonial opening of Parliament, the king said the U.K.'

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, May 6th: Trump threatens bombing if Iran doesn't reopen strait; China raises Iran war diplomacy before Trump-Xi summit; TV pioneer Ted Turner, founder of CNN, dies at 87; Klimt ceiling paintings displayed in Vienna theater.

video:video.video:title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff with Fujimori and Sánchez
video:video.video:description: Peruvian electoral authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

video:video.video:title: Rihanna arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Rihanna arrives at the Met Gala in custom Maison Margiela with A$AP Rocky.

video:video.video:title: Austria’s Cosmó says Eurovision is going well
video:video.video:description: Cosmó, who represents host country Austria in this year's Eurovision Song Contest, says that he's heard great things from other participants and everyone is trying to focus on the music.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories May 7
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Thursday, May 7th: Court releases Jeffery Epstein note; Iran says it’s reviewing latest proposal to end war; Secretary of State Marco Rubio to meet with the Pope; Hantavirus patients evacuated from cruise ship.

video:video.video:title: Chinese people share mixed feelings ahead of Trump's visit to China
video:video.video:description: Chinese people shared mixed feelings and expectations on Monday ahead of U.S. President Donald Trump's meeting with President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Indonesian woman killed after Mount Dukono eruption as rescuers search for 2 missing climbers
video:video.video:description: Two Singaporean climbers remain missing as rescue operations continue amid high volcanic activity after Mount Dukono erupted on the remote island of Halmahera.

video:video.video:title: Chinese President Xi welcomes visiting US President Trump in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Chinese President Xi Jinping hosts a welcoming ceremony for visiting US President Donald Trump ahead of a bilateral meeting.

video:video.video:title: Federal authorities arrest 18 for alleged drug distribution around Los Angeles park
video:video.video:description: Federal authorities said they arrested 18 people Wednesday on charges related to selling illicit drugs like fentanyl and methamphetamine around a Los Angeles park.

video:video.video:title: Romania performer defends 'Choke Me' song that has stirred Eurovision contest controversy
video:video.video:description: Romania's Eurovision singer reacted Sunday to the controversy around her team's song “Choke Me", their entry for this year’s Eurovision, as her and her team prepared to fly to Vienna for the singing contest final.

video:video.video:title: Hungary’s Péter Magyar sworn in as prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule
video:video.video:description: Hungary's Péter Magyar took his oath of office on Saturday to become the country's new prime minister, ending Viktor Orbán's 16 years of autocratic rule.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Raskin calls Trump's $1.7B IRS settlement his latest 'slush fund'
video:video.video:description: The lead Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee is calling the Trump administration's creation of a $1.7 billion fund to compensate allies of the Republican president who believe they were mistreated by the Biden-era Justice Department a "slush fund."

video:video.video:title: Doc featuring Sandra Oh explores Asian American, Pacific Islander identities
video:video.video:description: Timed for release during AAPI Heritage Month, documentary film “The A List: 15 Stories from Asian and Pacific Diasporas” has dropped on HBO Max. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Air Force trainer jet crashes, pilots eject safely
video:video.video:description: A U.S. Air Force training jet crashed Tuesday in west Alabama with both pilots ejecting safely, military officials said.

video:video.video:title: Kevin Hart at his roast: 'There's only one me'
video:video.video:description: Kevin Hart promises to return fire at comics who insult him at Netflix's "The Roast of Kevin Hart." (May 10)

video:video.video:title: "Seductive and accessible," Himid's work brightens the British Pavilion at the Venice Biennale.
video:video.video:description: Questions about identity, migration and belonging take centre stage at the Venice Biennale's British Pavilion, where artist Lubaina Himid is representing the United Kingdom with a major exhibition of new paintings.

video:video.video:title: Mýa returns with her first album in 8 years, the throwback 'Retrospect'
video:video.video:description: R&B singer Mýa is releasing "Retrospect," her tenth studio album and first full-length in eight years.

video:video.video:title: Handwritten letters in a 40-year-old pen pal relationship still goes on strong
video:video.video:description: Associated Press reporter Holly Ramer shares why she continues to handwrite letters to her New Zealand pen pal of 40 years. (AP video: Holly Ramer)

video:video.video:title: Trump: 'Very good talks' with Iran over the past 24 hours
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says the U.S. has "had very good talks" with Iran over the last 24 hours, adding "it's very possible we'll make a deal."

video:video.video:title: Handwriting on newly released note matches one found after Jeffrey Epstein's his death, expert says
video:video.video:description: A note that a former cellmate said he discovered after Jeffrey Epstein's first suspected jail suicide attempt was penned by the same person as a note that authorities found in the millionaire sex offender's cell after he killed himself three weeks later, handwriting expert says.

video:video.video:title: Drug counselor who delivered ketamine to Matthew Perry gets 2 years
video:video.video:description: Erik Fleming, the licensed drug addiction counselor who delivered “Friends” star Matthew Perry the doses of ketamine that killed him, has been sentenced to two years in prison.

video:video.video:title: Cardi B arrives at Met Gala
video:video.video:description: Cardi B makes her debut at the 2026 Met Gala.

video:video.video:title: Israel military destroys residential buildings in Gaza City after ordering residents to leave
video:video.video:description: Israel’s military destroyed residential buildings in Gaza Strip late Friday, after ordering residents to leave.

video:video.video:title: Plane carrying passengers from hantavirus ship lands in Nebraska
video:video.video:description: A plane carrying American passengers, who were evacuated from a hantavirus-hit cruise ship, landed in Omaha, Nebraska early Monday.

video:video.video:title: Trump said he didn’t ask China's Xi ‘for any favors’ on Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Friday he did not ask Chinese President Xi Jinping “for any favors” on Iran during their Beijing summit, insisting the U.S.

video:video.video:title: 'We've just hit somebody': Pilot tells ATC as plane hits person on Denver airport runway
video:video.video:description: The accident sparked an engine fire and forced passengers to evacuate. “We're stopping on the runway,” the pilot could be heard telling air traffic controllers, according to the site ATC.com.

video:video.video:title: Man gets life in prison for firebombing Colorado demonstration
video:video.video:description: A man was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole after pleading guilty Thursday to killing one person and injuring a dozen others in a 2025 firebombing attack on a demonstration in Boulder, Colorado, in support of Israeli hostages in Gaza.

video:video.video:title: Demi Moore: AI can never replace true art
video:video.video:description: Cannes Film Festival jury member Demi Moore says AI "isn't anything to fear" as it can never replace what "true art comes from." (May 12)

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike hits Lebanese coastal village of Saksakieh
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike that destroyed a house in the coastal village of Saksakieh in southern Lebanon killed four people and injured 33 others, the Lebanese health ministry said on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Death penalty opponents rally ahead of possible 600th Texas execution
video:video.video:description: Opponents of the death penalty gathered outside the Texas Capitol on Tuesday to urge Gov. Greg Abbott to halt the scheduled execution of Edward Busby, which would mark the 600th execution carried out in Texas since 1982.

video:video.video:title: Footage said to show Ukrainian sea drone approaching Russian tanker in the Black Sea
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Sunday that Ukrainian forces had hit two Russian tankers near the entrance of the Black Sea port of Novorossiysk.

video:video.video:title: Final evacuations of people from hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife wrap up
video:video.video:description: The last remaining passengers on a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak disembarked Monday and boarded flights to more than 20 countries to enter quarantine.

video:video.video:title: Iran FM: Strait of Hormuz suffering 'first and most from US blockade'
video:video.video:description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi criticized the U.S. in a televised interview on Thursday, blaming tensions in the Strait of Hormuz on "U.S. aggression".

video:video.video:title: Flames and violins fuse in Finland's Eurovision performance
video:video.video:description: Eurovision Song Contest favorites, Finland's Linda Lampenius x Pete Parkkonen discuss getting special permission to play live violin, for their performance of "Flamethrower."

video:video.video:title: Cruise ship hit by hantavirus outbreak leaves Cape Verde waters
video:video.video:description: A cruise ship linked to a deadly hantavirus outbreak departed Praia, Cape Verde, on Wednesday with nearly 150 passengers and crew on board, continuing its journey toward Spain’s Canary Islands as health authorities raced to contain the spread of the disease.

video:video.video:title: Laid off Spirit flight attendant reacts to news of airline shutting down
video:video.video:description: Spirit Airlines announced it is going out of business after 34 years, saying Saturday it has started winding down operations.

video:video.video:title: People protest Tennessee’s new US House map that carves up a majority-Black district in Memphis
video:video.video:description: The new voting districts signed into law Thursday by Republican Gov. Bill Lee gives the GOP a chance to win all nine of the state’s congressional seats in the November midterm elections.

video:video.video:title: Mexico’s 3,400-year-old ulama ballgame endures against the odds
video:video.video:description: Mexico is embracing its ancient ballgame, ulama, as it prepares to co-host the 2026 FIFA World Cup. This 3,400-year-old sport, nearly erased during the Spanish conquest, is gaining attention alongside international soccer.

video:video.video:title: 'Extraordinary Attorney Woo' star Park Eun-bin shares career secrets
video:video.video:description: South Korean actor Park Eun-bin explains what has kept her going through 30 years in the industry, from child model to her latest role in "The WONDERfools." (May 18)

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian drone pilots train NATO troops
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian drone pilots were invited to train NATO forces in Sweden - and delivered a critical message to the alliance.

video:video.video:title: Janet Jackson makes rare appearance at Grammy Hall of Fame Gala
video:video.video:description: Janet Jackson made a rare appearance at the 2026 Grammy Hall of Fame Gala, where her landmark 1989 album "Rhythm Nation 1814" was inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame.

video:video.video:title: Hockey drama 'Off Campus' uses 'Bridgerton' template
video:video.video:description: New hockey romance series "Off Campus" has a model like "Bridgerton," where each season will focus on a different relationship. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Trump still mulling US arms sale to Taiwan
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he’s not yet made a determination on whether a major U.S. sale of arms to Taiwan can move forward, following his three-day visit to China.

video:video.video:title: Modi’s party takes control of India's West Bengal in key state election
video:video.video:description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s ruling Hindu nationalist party on Monday wrested control of the state of West Bengal, an opposition stronghold, in a key election.

video:video.video:title: King Charles III meets guests and enters the DJ booth at Buckingham Palace garden party
video:video.video:description: Briain's King Charles III held a party at Buckingham Palace gardens on Thursday to celebrate 50 years since the launch of his charity.

video:video.video:title: How a bird protection group bought a forest rich in biodiversity
video:video.video:description: A rare pocket of woodland in northeastern Serbia has been saved from being cut down. A bird protection society raised 8,000 euros ($9,500) in a crowdfunding campaign to buy Nightingale's Forest and protect the flora and fauna that live there.

video:video.video:title: What to know about US military presence in Europe as Trump seeks drawdown
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. would go “a lot further” than a reduction of 5,000 troops announced by the Pentagon. It is exposing a new rift between NATO allies.

video:video.video:title: Russia's Putin to visit China days after Trump met with President Xi
video:video.video:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin will visit China Tuesday and Wednesday this week, the Chinese Foreign Minister announced.

video:video.video:title: For foreign workers in the Middle East, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
video:video.video:description: For 15 years, Mohammad Abdullah Al Mamun worked in Saudi Arabia, sending money home to his family in one of the poorest areas of Bangladesh.

video:video.video:title: Hitting the high notes: how Switzerland keeps its yodelling tradition alive
video:video.video:description: Yodelling is alive and well in the Swiss mountains. Swiss yodelling takes two distinct forms - natural yodelling, and yodel songs.

video:video.video:title: Fuel shortages and poor quality gasoline drive a surge in electric cars in Bolivia
video:video.video:description: Tired of gasoline shortages and skyrocketing prices, Simón Huanca took matters into his own hands. The 53-year-old Indigenous artisan imported a Chinese electric car to navigate El Alto, Bolivia’s highest city, using the vehicle to transport both his family and the alpaca wool for his weaving worksh

video:video.video:title: Hegseth gets bipartisan grilling on rising costs of the Iran war and Trump's end game
video:video.video:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has faced tough questions from Republican and Democratic lawmakers about the Trump administration’s end game for the Iran war, the rising cost of the conflict and its impact on diminishing U.S. weapons stockpiles.

video:video.video:title: Adam Driver responds to Lena Dunham
video:video.video:description: Actor Adam Driver kept tight lipped when asked if he had a response for Lena Dunham after comments made about him by her in her book.

video:video.video:title: Rescuers search for people under the rubble after Russian attack on Kyiv
video:video.video:description: Russia attacked Ukraine’s capital with a mass drone and missile strike early Thursday morning. The attack killed at least one and injured 33 people, according to the local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Procession, pomp, and ceremony: King Charles III lays out UK government agenda
video:video.video:description: In a speech to lawmakers laced with all the pomp that accompanies the ceremonial opening of Parliament, the king said the U.K.'

video:video.video:title: FBI searches Virginia Senate leader's office as part of corruption probe, AP source says
video:video.video:description: The FBI searched the Virginia state Senate leader's office on Wednesday as part of a corruption investigation, a person familiar with the matter said.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainians seeking cultural escape from war’s brutality find comfort and resilience at Kyiv art fair
video:video.video:description: A contemporary art fair was held in the Ukrainian capital with an unusual premise: that art can help a society come to terms with what war has made normal.

video:video.video:title: All-Indigenous soccer team debuts with victory in Brazil
video:video.video:description: An all-Indigenous soccer team made its professional debut on Sunday in Rio de Janeiro, securing a victory in its first match. AP video by Mario Lobao, Diarlei Rodrigues)

video:video.video:title: 'The Great Moose Migration' inspires fans to follow the Swedish slow TV hit
video:video.video:description: Swedish slow TV hit “The Great Moose Migration” is back for its 8th season offering viewers a break from fast-paced hard news and social media.

video:video.video:title: Small study hints CAR-T cell therapy might help fight HIV
video:video.video:description: Scientists are supercharging patients' own immune cells to try to fight HIV without today's drugs. A small study is giving a hint that CAR-T cell therapy, already used for some cancers, might eventually work for HIV.

video:video.video:title: Lights, camera, selfie - contestants arrive for Eurovision
video:video.video:description: Artists from 35 countries are competing to win the Eurovision Song Contest, which culminates with a Grand Final in Vienna on Saturday. (May 11)

video:video.video:title: Ships still wait in Strait of Hormuz as U.S. and Iran appear to move closer to ending war
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of merchant ships remain bottled up in the Persian Gulf as the United States and Iran appear to be moving closer to an initial agreement to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Winnie Harlow begins Met Gala planning early, but keeps her look under wraps
video:video.video:description: Winnie Harlow kept her Met Gala look under wraps, offering no details, but said she begins thinking about the event long before invitations arrive. (May 1)

video:video.video:title: AP explains Iran's Foreign Minister visit to China
video:video.video:description: China’s foreign minister on Wednesday called for a comprehensive ceasefire in the Iran war, in comments that could inject new energy into stalled efforts to end the two-month conflict between the United States and Iran.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief 'in touch with ship's captain regularly' amid hantavirus outbreak
video:video.video:description: WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus thanked the operator of the MV Hondius cruise ship for its cooperation amid the hantavirus outbreak on board.

video:video.video:title: Hantavirus is on the rise in Argentina, where a stricken cruise ship began its journey
video:video.video:description: Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise.

video:video.video:title: ASEAN leaders' contingency plan upholding international law, sovereignty and freedom of navigation
video:video.video:description: Southeast Asian leaders plan to issue a contingency plan that upholds international law, sovereignty and freedom of navigation, according to a draft declaration seen Thursday by The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Russian glide bomb attack on Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia kills at least 10
video:video.video:description: Russian forces struck the Ukrainian city of Zaporizhzhia with glide bombs on Tuesday, killing at least 10 and wounding 18 others, according to local authorities.

video:video.video:title: Trump departs Washington for high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump departed Washington for Beijing Tuesday and his high-stakes summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: Person shot by law enforcement near Washington Monument, Secret Service says
video:video.video:description: The person’s condition and the circumstances surrounding the shooting around 15th Street and Independence Avenue were not immediately known.

video:video.video:title: Italy's president and prime minister visit Modena the day after car attack
video:video.video:description: Italian President, Sergio Mattarella, and Prime Minister, Giorgia Meloni, visited a hospital on Sunday treating those injured after a car collided with pedestrians in the city of Modena.

video:video.video:title: Pediatricians say schools need to make time for recess for kids of all ages
video:video.video:description: For the first time in more than a decade, a leading pediatricians group has put out new guidance about recess, saying it’s crucial for good health and good grades.

video:video.video:title: Pop-up arcade game in DC mocks Trump administration's handling of Iran war
video:video.video:description: The mini arcade is the latest piece from Secret Handshake, a resistance art group that has installed several pieces of artwork on the Mall to protest the Trump administration, including a Golden toilet.

video:video.video:title: Trump invites Xi to the White House during state dinner in Beijing
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump extended a Sept. 24 invitation to Chinese President Xi Jinping and his wife, Madame Peng, during his reciprocal toast at a state dinner in the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

video:video.video:title: Australian Jews tell antisemitism inquiry of surge in hate before Bondi Hanukkah massacre
video:video.video:description: All witnesses called to give evidence on Monday were Jewish Australians who recounted their experiences of hatred, some speaking under pseudonyms out of fear for their safety.

video:video.video:title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia as protesters allied with ex-leader Morales march on capital
video:video.video:description: Followers of Bolivia’s influential ex-President Evo Morales on Monday joined a massive protest movement fueled by the nation’s worst economic crisis in a generation.

video:video.video:title: Amid confrontation with the US, an Iranian museum exhibits anti-war art by American artists
video:video.video:description: While Tehran's streets had lined with anti-American billboards and posters amid the tension in the Middle East, Iranians turned out to visit an anti-war exhibition at one of the top museums.

video:video.video:title: Kendrick Lamar, Dr. Dre and will.i.am attend school groundbreaking ceremony
video:video.video:description: Kendrick Lamar returned to his alma mater Centennial High School in Compton, Calif., to attend a groundbreaking ceremony alongside Dr. Dre and will.i.am.

video:video.video:title: Digital workers in Gaza try to earn badly needed incomes and find a form of escape from adversities
video:video.video:description: Digital freelancing became popular more than a decade ago in Gaza. But traditional sectors shrank after Hamas seized control of the strip in 2007, as Israel’s intensified blockade devastated agriculture, manufacturing and other industries.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories May 4
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, May 4th: U.N. mission on a task force on opening the Strait of Hormuz has been postponed; fire breaks out at Broadway theater; authorities seek suspects in lakeside shooting in Oklahoma; fish and chip prices on the rise in the UK

video:video.video:title: Alabama legislators and residents protest special session to redraw voting districts
video:video.video:description: U.S. Rep. Terri Sewell showed solidarity with local legislators at a rally outside Alabama's State House. (AP Video by Kim Chandler)

video:video.video:title: 'Fast & Furious' cast reunite at Cannes Film Festival
video:video.video:description: Vin Diesel, Meadow Walker and the 'Fast & Furious' stars celebrated the 25th anniversary of the franchise with a special midnight screening. (May 13)

video:video.video:title: Damson Idris walks the 2026 Met Gala carpet
video:video.video:description: Damson Idris wears leather on the 2026 Met Gala carpet.

video:video.video:title: Iran soccer team departs for Turkey training camp ahead of World Cup
video:video.video:description: Iran's semi-official Tasnim news agency posted a video showing members of the country's soccer team signing Jerseys ahead of their trip.

video:video.video:title: Some Japanese snack packages are turning black-and-white as Iran war depletes ink supply
video:video.video:description: The packaging on some snacks in Japan is turning a somber black-and-white, as the war in Iran disrupts the supply of an ingredient used in colored ink.
